title: The Generators of a Colon Ideal with an Application to the Weak Lefschetz Property for Monomial Almost Complete Intersections in Three Variables
plain_text: Consider the ideal $I=(x^ a_1+t , y^ a_2+t , z^ a_3+t , x^ a_1 y^ a_2 z^ a_3 ) subseteq $ where $a_1, a_2, a_3, t$ satisfy the following three conditions: equation 0<a_1 <= a_2 <= a_3 <= 2(a_1+a_2),\ 3\ divides\ a_1+a_2+a_3, \ and \ t >= a_1+a_2+a_3 3 . equation If $(a_1, a_2, a_3, t)$ is not $(2, 9, 13, 9)$ or $(3, 7, 14, 9)$, then $/I$ fails WLP if and only if $t$ is even, $a_1+a_2+a_3$ is odd, and $a_1=a_2$ or $a_2=a_3$.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: Longstanding concrete criterion for failure of the Weak Lefschetz Property in level monomial almost complete intersections; important in commutative algebra but a focused classification problem.
viability_rationale: Concrete combinatorial/algebraic conjecture with many partial results and explicit reductions; remaining finite/open parameter cases seem approachable by current techniques within a few years.
rationale: The Cook–Nagel/Migliore–Miró‑Roig–Nagel conjecture is presented as an open conjecture; the paper makes progress but explicitly says the forward implication remains open.
evidence_snippet: Lines 155-161 and 165-166: Conjecture stated; "The forward implication of the conjecture is still open, although some cases have been established."

title: Cylinders in weighted Fano varieties
plain_text: Let $S subset P(a_0,a_1,a_2,a_3)$ be a quasi-smooth, well-formed del Pezzo hypersurface of degree $d$ defined over an algebraically closed field $$ of characteristic zero. Then $S$ admits an anti-canonical polar cylinder if and only if $d = a_i + a_j$ for some $i != j$.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: A clean geometric criterion characterizing anti-canonical cylinders on quasi-smooth weighted del Pezzo hypersurfaces; significant for classification and birational/unipotent geometry within the subfield.
viability_rationale: Authors and other works cover all but finitely many classes; reduction to finitely many cases makes complete verification highly likely in the near term.
rationale: Conjecture \ref{conj:Cyl-criterion} is proposed and supported by evidence and partial results; the authors reduce verification to finitely many classes but do not claim a complete proof.
evidence_snippet: Lines 1706-1710 and 1717-1720: Conjecture stated; "complete verification ... is reduced to studying the existence ... for a finite number of classes" and partial positive results cited.

title: Bohr sets in sumsets III: expanding difference sets and almost Bohr sets
body_tex:
\label{conj:ab_expanding_implies_kmf}
Every $\AlmostBohrBohr$ set supports a $\KMF$ mean.
plain_text: Every $$ set supports a $$ mean.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: Upgrades a combinatorial/ergodic structural notion (AlmostBohrBohr) to existence of a special invariant (KMF) mean; meaningful within additive combinatorics and dynamics with clear structural consequences (DiffBohr, vdC).
viability_rationale: Technical ergodic/Bohr-set statement with partial structural progress but no clear closing route; authors display obstacles to the natural approach, so resolution in 5 years is plausible but uncertain.
rationale: The paper lists this statement in the open questions and discusses approaches without proving it; it remains conjectural here.
evidence_snippet: Lines 3189–3193: 'From their definitions... we believe that AlmostBohrBohr implies the stronger condition: supporting a KMF mean.' followed by Conjecture asserting every AlmostBohrBohr set supports a KMF mean.

title: Twisted Arinkin transforms and derived categories of moduli spaces on Kuznetsov components
plain_text: zhang ] Let $X$ be a hyperkähler variety of K3$^ [n] $-type with $n >= 2$. Then, there exists a K3 category $ A_X$ such that $X$ is a moduli space of stable objects on $ A_X$, and a canonical Brauer class $_X in (X)$ such that there is an exact linear equivalence $$D^b(X, _X^ n ) A_X^ [n] .$$
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: A deep bridge between hyperkähler K3^[n]-type geometry, K3 categories, and twisted derived equivalences; proving it would unify major moduli/derived-category perspectives in algebraic geometry.
viability_rationale: Significant recent special-case progress (Picard-rank/generic cases) provides evidence, yet the full Zhang conjecture is broad and deep in hyperkähler/K3-category theory, making a general resolution in 5 years unlikely but possible.
rationale: The Zhang conjecture is cited as open; the paper proves many special cases and provides evidence but does not prove the full conjecture.
evidence_snippet: Lines 247–251, 256–263: 'Note that Corollary... can be viewed as evidence for the following conjecture by Zhang' then state Zhang's conjecture; paper gives partial results and evidence.

title: An asymptotically optimal bound for the concentration function of a sum of independent integer random variables
body_tex:
(Juškevičius~\cite{tjp})\label{conj.main}
  Let $X_1, \dots, X_n$ and $Y_1, \dots, Y_n$ be as in Theorem~\ref{thm.tse}.
  Then there are $a_1, \dots, a_n \in \{-1,1\}$
  such that $\conc(\sum_{i=1}^n X_i) \le \conc(\sum_{i=1}^n a_i Y_i)$.
plain_text: (Juškevičius ) Let $X_1, , X_n$ and $Y_1, , Y_n$ be as in Theorem . Then there are $a_1, , a_n in -1,1 $ such that $(_ i=1 ^n X_i) <= (_ i=1 ^n a_i Y_i)$.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: An exact Littlewood–Offord–type extremal inequality for integer sums is a meaningful, nontrivial refinement of an asymptotic result; it has clear structural consequences in concentration theory though largely specialist in scope.
viability_rationale: Asymptotic resolution in the same paper, strong use of inverse Littlewood–Offord and local limit techniques; remaining gap is finite/non-asymptotic and technical, plausibly closable within a few years.
rationale: The paper proves only an asymptotic (1+δ) version, not the exact inequality asserted by Juškevičius, so the exact conjecture remains unresolved.
evidence_snippet: Lines 429-434 state the conjecture; lines 451-454 note Theorem shows an asymptotic form (1+δ) but not the exact conjecture.

title: Cores and localizations of $(\infty,\infty)$-categories
body_tex:
\label{conj-image-weak}
  The image of the embedding
  $R\colon \Cat^L_\infty\hookrightarrow \Cat_\infty$
  consists precisely of the $\omega$-complete $\infty$-categories.
plain_text: The image of the embedding $R ^L_ _$ consists precisely of the $$-complete $$-categories.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: Characterizing the image of a canonical embedding of L-local ∞-categories would clarify foundational structure in higher category theory and reflectivity, a central structural problem in the field.
viability_rationale: Concrete equivalent formulation given; specialized higher-category technical obstacles remain, so moderate but uncertain chance of resolution in five years.
rationale: The authors pose Loubaton's statement as an open conjecture (image of R equals ω-complete ∞-categories) and do not prove it here.
evidence_snippet: Lines 2016-2021 state the conjecture and subsequent lemma rephrases it as an open question; it's listed under 'Open questions'.

title: Violating the All-or-Nothing Picture of Local Charges in Non-Hermitian Bosonic Chains
body_tex: For each natural class of one-dimensional nearest-neighbor systems, there exists a finite integer $M\ge 3$ such that, for every Hamiltonian in the class, the existence of an $M$-local charge is equivalent to the existence of $k$-local charges for all $M+1\le k\le N/2$.
plain_text: For each natural class of one-dimensional nearest-neighbor systems, there exists a finite integer $M >= 3$ such that, for every Hamiltonian in the class, the existence of an $M$-local charge is equivalent to the existence of $k$-local charges for all $M+1 <= k <= N/2$.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: A proposed universal finite-range diagnostic for local conserved charges generalizes an integrability test; important to many-body integrability but chiefly consequential within that subfield.
viability_rationale: Paper gives explicit counterexamples to naive universality and a classification in a class; general statement is plausible but broad and class-dependent, moderate solvability.
rationale: The paper disproves universality of M=3 and exhibits classes with M=5, but does not prove the generalized statement for all natural classes, so it remains open.
evidence_snippet: Lines 1540-1546 formulate the Generalized Grabowski--Mathieu test as a conjecture after showing counterexamples to the M=3 universality.

title: Toroidal families and averages of $L$-functions, II: cubic moments
body_tex:
\label{conjpiercetriple} Let $a,b,c$ be non-zero integers with $a,b,c$ setwise coprime and $d\geq 1$ another integer.
 
  For any prime $q\geq 3$, there exists $\eta_0>0$ (depending on $a,b,c,d$ but not on $q$) such that for $L,M,N\geq 1$  and  $\eps>0$, one has 
  $$\frac{N_{a,b,c,d}(L,M,N;q)}{\sqrt{LMN}}\ll_{\eps,a,b,c,d} q^\eps\Bigl( \frac{\sqrt{LMN}}{q}+(LMN)^{-\eta_0}\Bigr).$$
plain_text: Let $a,b,c$ be non-zero integers with $a,b,c$ setwise coprime and $d >= 1$ another integer. For any prime $q >= 3$, there exists $_0>0$ (depending on $a,b,c,d$ but not on $q$) such that for $L,M,N >= 1$ and $>0$, one has $$ N_ a,b,c,d (L,M,N;q) LMN _ ,a,b,c,d q^( LMN q +(LMN)^ -_0 ).$$
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: A meaningful analytic-number-theory conjecture: bounding monomial congruence counts in small boxes underpins cubic moment asymptotics for Dirichlet L-values; important within the subfield though not a flagship open problem.
viability_rationale: Strong partial results and methods (trace functions, stratification) approach the critical range; authors indicate concrete further work, but full uniform bound remains technically demanding.
rationale: The paper states this counting conjecture P(a,b,c,d) and only proves special cases (e.g. a=b); the general statement is assumed for a theorem and left for future work.
evidence_snippet: Lines 738-746: 'the general case rests currently on a conjectural statement P(a,b,c,d) (which we will prove for a=b)'; Lines 756-769: 'Assuming this conjecture we have Theorem...'

title: Toroidal families and averages of $L$-functions, II: cubic moments
plain_text: Let $a,b,c$ be non-zero setwise coprime integers and $d >= 1$ be another integer. For $q$ a prime and $L,M,N >= 1$ let $$ N_ a,b,c,d (L,M,N;q):= | (l,m,n) sim L x M x N,\ (l^am^bn^c)^d 1 q,\ l^am^bn^c=1 |. $$ There exists $_0>0$ (depending on $a,b,c,d$ but not on $q$) such that for any $>0$, one has equation N_ a,b,c,d (L,M,N;q) LMN _ ,a,b,c,d q^( LMN q +(LMN)^ -_0 ). equation
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: Same monomial-congruence counting conjecture as above; it directly controls error terms in cubic-moment evaluations for L-functions, a substantive specialist problem in analytic number theory.
viability_rationale: Same statement as Conjecture 865: significant progress near critical ranges and explicit analytic methods exist, yet remaining uniform cases are nontrivial.
rationale: The same conjectural bound on N_{a,b,c,d}(L,M,N;q) is restated and discussed; the authors provide partial ranges and plan further study, not a full proof.
evidence_snippet: Lines 2655-2660: statement of conjecture; Lines 2676-2680: 'techniques ... allow us to pass this critical range ... when (a,b,c) is good ... We plan to pursue the study of Conjecture P(a,b,c,d) in a later work.'

title: Pairwise Negative Correlation for Uniform Spanning Subgraphs of the Complete Graph
body_tex:
\label{conj: p-NC for random cluster model}
	The random-cluster measures $\phi_{p,q}=\phi_{p,q}(G)$ on a finite graph $G=(V,E)$ with parameter $q\in(0,1)$ satisfy the p-NC property.
plain_text: The random-cluster measures $_ p,q =_ p,q (G)$ on a finite graph $G=(V,E)$ with parameter $q in (0,1)$ satisfy the p-NC property.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: Longstanding conjecture that random-cluster measures with q in (0,1) have pairwise negative correlation; central to negative dependence and statistical mechanics with wide structural consequences if resolved.
viability_rationale: Longstanding, broad conjecture about random-cluster negative dependence for q<1; widely open with no clear imminent general resolution.
rationale: The p-NC property for random-cluster measures with q in (0,1) is cited as a conjecture from earlier work and used as motivation; this paper does not prove it.
evidence_snippet: Lines 466-476 and 476-478: conjecture statement attributed to Kahn2000, Grimmett_Winkler2004; lines 481-483: 'Our motivation stems from the conjectured validity ... with q<1.'

title: Pairwise Negative Correlation for Uniform Spanning Subgraphs of the Complete Graph
body_tex:
\label{conj: NC for UF}
	If $G$ is a finite connected graph and $\UF$ is the uniform forest measure on $G$, then  $\UF$  satisfies the p-NC property.
plain_text: If $G$ is a finite connected graph and $$ is the uniform forest measure on $G$, then $$ satisfies the p-NC property.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: Conjecture that uniform forest measures on any finite connected graph satisfy p-NC is a natural, concrete special case of the random-cluster question with notable combinatorial/probabilistic implications.
viability_rationale: Special-case (uniform forests) has substantial partial progress (numerics, large-n proofs, inequalities). Full general finite-graph claim remains challenging but more approachable than the full random-cluster conjecture.
rationale: The uniform-forest p-NC conjecture is presented as an open conjecture; the paper cites partial results (numerical checks, large-n proofs) but does not prove it in full generality.
evidence_snippet: Lines 529-536: 'can be viewed as a special case... Grimmett and Winkler verified numerically... Stark proved ... for K_n when n is sufficiently large; Brändén and Huh proved related inequality.'

title: The complexity of finite smooth words over binary alphabets
body_tex:
\label{mixedCf}
	Over mixed alphabets, every smooth word $x$ satisfies $\mathscr{L}(x) = \mathcal{C}_f^\infty$, and in particular $p_x(n) = p_{\mathcal{C}^\infty}(n) = p_{\mathcal{C}_f^\infty}(n)$.
plain_text: Over mixed alphabets, every smooth word $x$ satisfies $L(x) = C_f^$, and in particular $p_x(n) = p_ C^ (n) = p_ C_f^ (n)$.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: Statement about languages of smooth words over mixed alphabets is a focused combinatorics-on-words conjecture tied to recurrence; interesting within the niche but limited broader impact.
viability_rationale: A combinatorics-on-words conjecture with concrete combinatorial structure; progress is plausible but nontrivial, with moderate chance of resolution in five years.
rationale: The conjecture on languages of individual smooth words over mixed alphabets is stated but not proved; the paper proves related set-level equality only, not the per-word claim.
evidence_snippet: Lines 274-278: conjecture statement; Line 296: 'we will show in Th1 that L(C^infty)=C_f^infty', which is weaker than the conjecture for every individual smooth word.

title: The complexity of finite smooth words over binary alphabets
body_tex:
\label{CfTheta}
	Over $\mathpzc{A} = \{a,b\}$, we have $p_{\mathcal{C}_f^\infty}(n) = \Theta(n^\rho)$ where $\rho = \frac{\log(a+b)}{\log\left(\frac{a+b}{2}\right)}$.
plain_text: Over $A = a,b $, we have $p_ C_f^ (n) = (n^)$ where $ = (a+b) (a+b 2 ) $.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: Sing's conjecture on polynomial growth exponent for f-smooth word complexity is a substantive, well-known problem in combinatorics on words, with implications for morphic-word classification.
viability_rationale: Substantial partial results: proven for even alphabets, lower bounds general, improved upper bounds for odd; remaining gap is technical and plausibly closable soon.
rationale: The growth-rate conjecture p_{C_f^infty}(n)=Θ(n^ρ) is the main conjecture; the paper proves it for even alphabets and partial bounds elsewhere, leaving the full statement unresolved.
evidence_snippet: Lines 300-304: conjecture statement; Lines 311-318 and abstract: authors prove it for even alphabets and obtain partial lower/upper bounds for other cases.

title: Rigidity of Critical Point Metrics under some Ricci curvature constraints
body_tex: CPE metrics are Einstein.
plain_text: CPE metrics are Einstein.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: Besse's CPE conjecture (CPE metrics are Einstein) is a central, longstanding Riemannian-geometry problem with significant structural consequences; many partial results but the full conjecture remains open.
viability_rationale: Besse's CPE→Einstein conjecture is classical with many conditional partial results; full unconditional resolution seems unlikely within five years.
rationale: The CPE conjecture ('CPE metrics are Einstein') is classical and is assumed/open; the paper proves it under additional hypotheses but not in full generality.
evidence_snippet: Lines 86-91: Besse's conjecture 'CPE metrics are Einstein'; Lines 123-126 and later: authors prove the conjecture under traceless Ricci conditions.

title: Rigidity of Critical Point Metrics under some Ricci curvature constraints
body_tex: A \emph{CPE metric} is spherical.
plain_text: A CPE metric is spherical.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: Refined Besse conjecture (CPE metrics are spherical) is equivalently deep and consequential in differential geometry; resolving it would classify nontrivial CPE metrics and generalize many partial rigidity results.
viability_rationale: Refined spherical form of the CPE conjecture shares the same longstanding difficulty and partial-progress profile; near-term full resolution unlikely.
rationale: The refined CPE conjecture ('CPE metric is spherical') is stated as open; the paper gives partial/sufficient-condition results but does not settle the full conjecture.
evidence_snippet: Lines 105-108: 'Now we can give a refined version ... A CPE metric is spherical.' Lines 115-124: many verified special cases; authors prove results under additional traceless Ricci assumptions.

title: The moduli space of dynamical spherically symmetric black hole spacetimes and the extremal threshold
plain_text: Let $ C$ and $ M$ be as above such that the extremal Kerr--Newman black hole $g_ M_0,a_0,e_0 $ arises from an initial data set $_0 in M$. There exists a neighborhood $ M_nbhd$ of $_0$ with the following properties: Universality of the Kerr--Newman family: If $ in M_black cap M_nbhd$, then $ S[]$ converges to a member of the Kerr--Newman family in an appropriate sense. Moreover, $ M_nbhd=( M_black M_non) cap M_nbhd$, i.e., no naked singularities arise from initial data in $ M_nbhd$. Isologous foliation and threshold property: There exists a $C^1$ foliation of $ M_black cap M_nbhd$ by hypersurfaces $ M_stab^ subset M_nbhd$, $ in [0,1]$, such that if $ in M_stab^$, then $ S[]$ converges to a Kerr--Newman black hole with parameters $(M,a,e)$ satisfying $ = a^2+e^2/M$. The extremal hypersurface $ M^1_stab$ is the local black hole threshold, $ M_black cap M_nbhd= M^1_stab$, and locally separates $ M_black$ and $ M_non$. Here, unlike thm:intro-foliation, $$ is not a signed quantity and it is implicit that $ M_stab^$ can be empty for $$ not sufficiently close to $1$, as the hypersurfaces can simply exit $ M_nbhd$. For any $ in M^1_stab$, the maximal development does not contain any strictly trapped surfaces. Scaling laws: For a generic one-parameter family of black hole initial data $ _p _ p in [0,1] $ crossing the extremal threshold, the final parameter ratio, area of the event horizon, and temperature scale as in thm:intro-scaling. In symmetry, the location of the event horizon scales in the same way as well. Horizon instabilities: A generic solution lying on $ M_stab^1$ will experience some form of growing hair on its event horizon for all time (see below on what the nature of these instabilities will be in various settings). A generic near-threshold solution will experience a transient instability on the timescale of its inverse final temperature.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: Central nonlinear-gravitational PDE conjecture about the local moduli of extremal Kerr–Newman, foliation/threshold structure, universal scaling and instabilities; resolving it would significantly impact black hole stability and cosmic censorship research.
viability_rationale: Generalization outside spherical symmetry; proved here only in symmetry. Vacuum/non-symmetric stability problems remain deep technical obstacles, so full conjecture unlikely within five years.
rationale: The paper explicitly poses this general Kerr--Newman threshold conjecture as an open conjecture; the authors only prove the analogous picture in the spherically symmetric (Reissner–Nordström) setting, not the full Kerr--Newman generality.
evidence_snippet: “We now make the following conjecture… [Extremal event horizon threshold conjecture]” (lines 614–626). The paper summarizes this as a generalization of results proved only in symmetry (line 628–630).

title: The moduli space of dynamical spherically symmetric black hole spacetimes and the extremal threshold
plain_text: Let $ M$ be as above, with a matter model and symmetry combination such that: [0.] Extremal Kerr--Newman can form: An exact extremal Kerr--Newman event horizon can form in finite time from an initial data set $ in M$. Then the following holds: The third law is false: There exist data $ in M$, whose evolution $ S[]$ describes a stationary subextremal apparent horizon forming in finite time, which at a later finite time evolves into an exactly extremal Kerr--Newman event horizon. Moreover, this can be achieved in true gravitational collapse, i.e., the event horizon $ H^+$ does not intersect the Cauchy data hypersurface. Extremal critical collapse can occur: There exist interpolating families of initial data $ _p _ p in [0,1] $ for which the critical solution $ S[_ p_* ]$ forms an asymptotically extremal Kerr--Newman black hole. Structure of the threshold: Let $_ p_* $ be critical as in the previous point. Then there exists a neighborhood $ M_nbhd$ of $_ p_* $ such that all $ in M_nbhd$ either (a) collapse to form an asymptotically Kerr--Newman black hole or (b) lie in $ M_non$. Moreover, there exists a $C^1$ isologous foliation by hypersurfaces $ M_stab^ subset M_nbhd$, $ in [0,1]$, such that $ M_black cap M_nbhd= M^1_stab$. Scaling laws: For a generic one-parameter family of black hole initial data $ _p _ p in [0,1] $ crossing the extremal threshold, the final parameter ratio, area of the event horizon, and temperature scale as in thm:intro-scaling. In symmetry, the location of the event horizon scales in the same way as well. Moreover, when $_p in M_non$, the timescale on which $ S[_p]$ resembles extremal Kerr--Newman is $ sim |p-p_*|^ -1/2 $. Horizon instabilities: A generic solution lying on $ M_stab^1$ will experience some form of growing hair on its event horizon for all time (see below on what the nature of these instabilities will be in various settings). A generic near-threshold solution will experience a transient instability on the timescale of its inverse final temperature.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: Asserts extremal critical collapse, falsity of a form of the third law, and universal scaling for critical solutions — a deep statement in gravitational collapse and critical phenomena with broad implications in GR.
viability_rationale: Extremal critical collapse partially confirmed in specific models, but full generality (especially vacuum) has major gaps; some progress likely but full resolution seems unlikely soon.
rationale: The extremal critical collapse conjecture is stated as a general conjecture; authors note some parts are confirmed in special models, but crucial parts (e.g. part 2) remain open and hinge on other conjectures.
evidence_snippet:
“For the spherically symmetric EMCSF model, parts 0 and 1 … were confirmed … In 
[Corollary 3] … It is natural to conjecture that this example lies on the threshold, but it is not clear to us how to prove that statement. Thus, part 2 remains open” (lines 774–776).

title: The moduli space of dynamical spherically symmetric black hole spacetimes and the extremal threshold
body_tex:
\label{conj:closing}
For \ul{generic} $\mathcal S[\Psi_{p_*}]$, conditioned on being critical in the sense of \cref{def:ECC} for the EMCSF model, the Cauchy horizon $\mathcal{CH}^+$ arising from future timelike infinity $i^+$ smoothly closes off the spacetime. In other words, Figure 1 of \cite{van2023breakdown} holds true, in sharp contrast to the generic subextremal case.
plain_text: For generic $ S[_ p_* ]$, conditioned on being critical in the sense of def:ECC for the EMCSF model, the Cauchy horizon $CH^+$ arising from future timelike infinity $i^+$ smoothly closes off the spacetime. In other words, Figure 1 of holds true, in sharp contrast to the generic subextremal case.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: Predicts smooth closure of the Cauchy horizon in generic extremal critical EMCSF solutions — a specific but meaningful claim about interior singularity structure and strong cosmic censorship in a concrete matter model.
viability_rationale: Concrete EMCSF interior statement with supporting examples and partial results; mass-inflation/genericity issues remain but are more focused and plausibly resolved in near term.
rationale: The closing conjecture for generic critical EMCSF solutions is presented as an open conjecture; the paper notes one example satisfying it but does not prove the general statement.
evidence_snippet: Conjecture stated: “For generic S[Psi_{p_*}] … the Cauchy horizon CH^+ ... smoothly closes” (lines 780–782). The authors note an example satisfies the conclusion but leave the conjecture open (line 784).

title: On R-disjoint graphs: a generalization of almost bipartite non-König-Egerváry graphs
body_tex:
The determinant of the adjacency matrix $A(G)$
	of an $R$-disjoint graph admits a factorization (Schur-type functions,
	see \cite{h2,s3}) in terms of the flower decomposition $B(G),R_{1},\dots,R_{k}$, that is,
	\[
	\det A(G)=\det A(G[B(G)])\prod_{i=1}^{k}\det A(G[R_{i}]).
	\]
plain_text: The determinant of the adjacency matrix $A(G)$ of an $R$-disjoint graph admits a factorization (Schur-type functions, see ) in terms of the flower decomposition $B(G),R_ 1 ,,R_ k $, that is, \[ A(G)= A(G[B(G)])_ i=1 ^ k A(G[R_ i ]). \]
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: Spectral factorization for a new structural decomposition is a meaningful algebraic-graph statement with useful eigenvalue consequences, but it is specialized to the R-disjoint family and not a broad flagship problem.

title: The Flint Hills Series, Mixed Tate Motives, and a Criterion for the Irrationality Measure of $π$
body_tex:
\label{conj:explicit}
Assume $\mu(\pi)\leq 5/2$.
There exist $c_{1},c_{2}\in\Q$ such that
\[
  R_{1}^{*}
  = c_{1}\,\zeta(3) + c_{2}\,L(3,\chi_{-3}),
\]
and consequently
\[
  S = \Bigl(\tfrac{4}{3}+\tfrac{c_{1}}{3}\Bigr)\zeta(3)
    + \frac{c_{2}}{3}\,L(3,\chi_{-3}).
\]
The numerical evidence of Section~\ref{sec:numerical} is consistent
with $c_{1}$ and $c_{2}$ being small rationals, but their exact
values require a complete motivic cycle computation.
plain_text: Assume $() <= 5/2$. There exist $c_ 1 ,c_ 2 in $ such that \[ R_ 1 ^ * = c_ 1 \,(3) + c_ 2 \,L(3,_ -3 ), \] and consequently \[ S = (4 3 + c_ 1 3 )(3) + c_ 2 3 \,L(3,_ -3 ). \] The numerical evidence of Section is consistent with $c_ 1 $ and $c_ 2 $ being small rationals, but their exact values require a complete motivic cycle computation.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: A concrete motivic period identity linking the Flint Hills series to ζ(3) and L(3,χ_{-3}), conditional on μ(π)≤5/2; deep within mixed-Tate/K-theory but specialized to a single period.
viability_rationale: Depends on the strong, currently unproven bound μ(π)≤5/2 and on hard explicit motivic cycle computations in K-theory; both obstacles make a full resolution within five years unlikely.
rationale: The paper states this as a conjecture and does not determine the exact rational coefficients; it notes a complete motivic cycle computation is required and the claim is conditional on μ(π)≤5/2.
evidence_snippet: Lines 559-566: conjecture asserts R1* = c1 ζ(3)+c2 L(3,χ_{-3}). Lines 571-573: exact values require a complete motivic cycle computation.

title: Exploring Collatz Dynamics with Human-LLM Collaboration
body_tex:
\label{conj:equidist}
For every odd $n_0$, the sequence of gap-return residues
$a_i = T^i(n_0) \bmod 2^M$ is equidistributed modulo $2^M$,
\emph{uniformly in~$M$}: there exists a function
$M(N) \to \infty$ such that
\[
  \|\mu_{\mathrm{orb},N} - \mu_U\|_{\mathrm{TV}} \to 0
  \quad \text{as } N \to \infty
  \qquad \text{modulo } 2^{M(N)},
\]
where $\mu_{\mathrm{orb},N} = \frac{1}{N}\sum_{i=1}^N
\delta_{a_i}$ and $\mu_U$ is the uniform distribution on the
admissible residue classes modulo~$2^{M(N)}$.

In particular, this implies fixed-modulus equidistribution
(take $M(N) = M$ constant) and provides the tail control
needed to pass from truncated to full orbitwise means
(see Theorem~\ref{thm:reduction}).
plain_text: For every odd $n_0$, the sequence of gap-return residues $a_i = T^i(n_0) 2^M$ is equidistributed modulo $2^M$, uniformly in $M$: there exists a function $M(N) to $ such that \[ \|_ orb,N - _U\|_ TV to 0 as N to modulo 2^ M(N) , \] where $_ orb,N = 1 N _ i=1 ^N _ a_i $ and $_U$ is the uniform distribution on the admissible residue classes modulo $2^ M(N) $. In particular, this implies fixed-modulus equidistribution (take $M(N) = M$ constant) and provides the tail control needed to pass from truncated to full orbitwise means (see Theorem ).
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: Implies the Collatz conjecture; proving uniform 2-adic equidistribution of orbit residues is a deep dynamical/arithmetic problem with flagship-level consequences across number theory and dynamics.
viability_rationale: Equivalent to Collatz-style deep dynamics; key orbitwise hypotheses remain open and the paper gives only a conditional reduction, so a full resolution in five years is very unlikely.
rationale: The paper states the Orbit Equidistribution Conjecture and uses it conditionally to imply Collatz, explicitly noting the key hypotheses remain open and not proved in this work.
evidence_snippet: Conjecture stated (Sec. 1340–1361). Theorem: Conjecture implies Collatz (Thm. reduction 1365–1368). Abstract: 'the key hypotheses remain open.'

title: Duality in mass-action networks
body_tex:
\label{conj:duality}
For conservative mass-action networks that do not have two species
with exactly the same rates and have at least a positive steady state, there 
is a duality relation between the sets of preclusters and
of maximal invariant polyhedral supports.
plain_text: For conservative mass-action networks that do not have two species with exactly the same rates and have at least a positive steady state, there is a duality relation between the sets of preclusters and of maximal invariant polyhedral supports.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: Posits a structural duality in mass-action network theory between preclusters and maximal invariant polyhedral supports; a conceptual bridge with potentially broad implications for CRN analysis.
viability_rationale: Partial structural results exist (conserved quantities vs internal cycles); plausible but requires new conceptual links between preclusters and polyhedral supports—moderate chance of resolution soon.
rationale: The conjecture relating preclusters and maximal invariant polyhedral supports is posed explicitly after proving a related but different duality theorem; no proof or resolution is given.
evidence_snippet: Lines 702-722: Theorem proves conserved quantities dual to internal cycles, then lines 714-722 state the conjecture about duality between preclusters and maximal invariant polyhedral supports.

title: Sampling Colorings with Fixed Color Class Sizes
body_tex: For any graph $G$ with maximum degree $\Delta$ and $\vec{n}$ satisfying $\| \vec{n} \|_1 = n$ and $n_i \leq \left\lfloor \dfrac{n}{\Delta + 1} \right\rfloor$ for all $i \in [q]$, there exists an $\vec{n}$-coloring of $G$.
plain_text: For any graph $G$ with maximum degree $$ and $n$ satisfying $\| n \|_1 = n$ and $n_i <= n + 1 $ for all $i in [q]$, there exists an $n$-coloring of $G$.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: A natural and strong generalization of the Hajnal–Szemerédi theorem about constrained color-class sizes; resolving it would advance graph coloring theory significantly.
viability_rationale: A broad, strong generalization of Hajnal–Szemerédi with hard global constraints; no clear narrowing of the gap provided, so unlikely to be settled in five years.
rationale: The authors state this generalization of Hajnal–Szemerédi as their own conjecture and do not prove it (they prove related results and special cases only).
evidence_snippet: Lines 373-378: 'We conjecture that a bound ... suffices. This conjecture generalizes the theorem of Hajnal and Szemerédi.' Conjecture statement follows; no proof provided.

title: Sampling Colorings with Fixed Color Class Sizes
body_tex: There is a polynomial-time approximate sampling algorithm for uniform $\vec n$-colorings in graphs of maximum degree $\Delta$, for $\vec n$ satisfying $n_i \leq \left\lfloor \dfrac{n}{2\Delta } \right\rfloor$ for all $i \in [q]$
plain_text: There is a polynomial-time approximate sampling algorithm for uniform $ n$-colorings in graphs of maximum degree $$, for $ n$ satisfying $n_i <= n 2 $ for all $i in [q]$
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: An algorithmic conjecture to sample highly skewed color-class size vectors in bounded-degree graphs; important for sampling theory though more technical and tied to known barriers (q≈2Δ).
viability_rationale: Algorithmic/analytic barrier at q≈2Δ is identified and authors suggest a concrete stronger existence regime; extending methods to sampling at 2Δ scale seems plausible but nontrivial.
rationale: The algorithmic conjecture for sampling highly skewed colorings is explicitly posed and not proved in the paper; authors describe it as a plausible further goal.
evidence_snippet: Lines 383-391: 'We also conjecture that it should be possible to sample colorings that are much more skewed' followed by the stated sampling conjecture; no proof given.

title: A reverse isoperimetric inequality in three-dimensional space forms
body_tex: Let $K \subset M^n(c)$ be a $\lambda$-convex body, $n \ge 2$, and let $L \subset M^n(c)$ be a $\lambda$-convex \emph{lens}. If $|\partial K| = |\partial L|$, then $|K| \ge |L|$, with equality if and only if $K$ is a $\lambda$-convex lens.
plain_text: Let $K subset M^n(c)$ be a $$-convex body, $n >= 2$, and let $L subset M^n(c)$ be a $$-convex lens. If $| K| = | L|$, then $|K| >= |L|$, with equality if and only if $K$ is a $$-convex lens.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: Borisenko's reverse isoperimetric conjecture in higher dimensions is a central, deep problem in convex and geometric analysis with significant structural consequences; n≥4 remains open and important.
viability_rationale: Higher-dimensional Borisenko conjecture (n≥4) is a longstanding geometric problem; recent progress at low dimensions helps, but full higher-dimensional resolution looks unlikely within five years.
rationale: The Borisenko Conjecture as stated for all dimensions n≥2 remains unresolved in dimensions n≥4; the paper proves the n=3 (nonzero curvature) case only.
evidence_snippet: Lines 239-247: Conjecture stated; lines 243-244 note conjecture resolved for n=2 and n=3 (recent work), but 'For dimensions n ≥ 4, the conjecture remains completely open.'

title: A note on hyperseparating set systems
body_tex:
For any $k$, if $n$ is sufficiently large, then
    we have $f(n,k)= \min \{m:\binom{m}{k}\ge n\}$.
plain_text: For any $k$, if $n$ is sufficiently large, then we have $f(n,k)= m:m k >= n $.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: An extremal set-system asymptotic equality conjecture; a natural combinatorial statement of moderate depth constrained to the specialty of separating/hyperseparating systems.
viability_rationale: An extremal set-system statement with the k=2 case proved and standard duality tools available; likely approachable by combinatorial/extremal methods for fixed k as n→∞.
rationale: The general formula for f(n,k) is conjectured for all k and large n; the paper proves the k=2 case only and does not resolve the general claim.
evidence_snippet: Lines 196-204: 'We conjecture that the upper bound is sharp for large n.' Conjecture (lines 198-200) followed by 'We can prove this conjecture for k=2.'

title: The Exact Erdős-Ko-Rado Theorem for 3-wise $t$-intersecting uniform families
plain_text: Let $ subset [n] k $ be $r$-wise $t$-intersecting, $(r-1)(n-k)>k-t$ and suppose that $|_1^k(n,r,t)| <= n-t k-t $. Then align || <= n-t k-t . align

interestingness_rationale: The uniform Exact EKR analogue is an important refinement of Frankl's conjecture; partially resolved cases exist (e.g. r=3), so it is substantial within extremal set theory though narrower than the full non-uniform conjecture.
viability_rationale: Uniform Exact EKR has substantial recent partial progress (specific r=3 and large-parameter cases); remaining cases are technical but may yield to current methods.
rationale: The uniform Exact EKR conjecture (Conj. 6.4) is stated and only partial cases (e.g. r=3 with large parameters) are proved in this paper; full conjecture remains open.
evidence_snippet: Lines 1189–1194 state Conjecture 6.4; line 1198 notes the authors proved the r=3, large-k case but not the full conjecture.

title: Validity of the Strong Version of the Union of Uniform Closed Balls Conjecture in the Plane
body_tex: {JCA2011}}] \label{strongconj} Let $S\subset\mathbb{R}^n$ be a nonempty and closed set satisfying the interior $r$-sphere condition for some $r>0$. Then $S$ is the union of closed balls with common radius $\frac{nr}{2\sqrt{n^2-1}}$.

title: Proof of 100 Euro Conjecture
body_tex:
\label{conj:l2}
	Let $A=(a_{ij})\in\R^{n\times n}$ with $n\ge 2$, and let $ r_i=(a_{i1},\dots,a_{in})$ denote the $i$th row of $A$. If
	\[
	\norm{ r_i}_2\ge \sqrt{n-1}
	\qquad (1\le i\le n),
	\]
	then there exists $x\in\R^n\setminus\{0\}$ such that
	\[
	\abs{Ax}\ge \abs{x}.
	\]

rationale: The B"unger--Seeger (200 Euro) conjecture is stated as stronger than Rump's; this paper proves the 100 Euro case only and gives weaker results for 200 Euro, so it remains open.

title: Haar-Type Measures on Topological Quasigroups and Kunen's Theorem
body_tex:
Let $Q$ be a locally compact Hausdorff topological quasigroup
satisfying the Moufang-type identity $(N1)$.
Assume that $Q$ admits a quasi-invariant measure
with modular cocycle $j$.

Then the cocycle collapses:
\[
j \equiv 1 .
\]

In particular, the translation geometry becomes
unimodular in the measure-theoretic sense.
plain_text: Let $Q$ be a locally compact Hausdorff topological quasigroup satisfying the Moufang-type identity $(N1)$. Assume that $Q$ admits a quasi-invariant measure with modular cocycle $j$. Then the cocycle collapses: \[ j 1 . \] In particular, the translation geometry becomes unimodular in the measure-theoretic sense.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: Proposes a measure-theoretic collapse (unimodularity) linking Moufang identities to quasi-invariant measures, offering a conceptual measure-theoretic form of Kunen's theorem.
viability_rationale: Abstract measure-theoretic collapse tied to Kunen's theorem; existence and rigidity of quasi-invariant measures and cocycle collapse appear deep and speculative, with no clear concrete route to resolution soon.
rationale: Author introduces the 'Modular Collapse Conjecture' as a new conjecture linking cocycle collapse to unimodularity; it is stated but not proved in the paper.
evidence_snippet: "Modular Collapse Conjecture... Then the cocycle collapses: j ≡ 1." (lines 922-931)

title: Classical and irregular Hodge numbers

label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: Equates two Landau–Ginzburg Hodge-number notions; central to Hodge theory and mirror symmetry. Equivalent to Hodge–Tate limiting Hodge structures, so resolution would have clear structural consequences across complex geometry.
viability_rationale: Specialized Hodge-theoretic statement with substantial partial reductions in the paper; non-degenerate cases resolved, but full general equality likely still requires deep Hodge/limiting-structure work.
rationale: Paper cites KKP conjecture, proves only an equivalent criterion (Hodge--Tate limiting Hodge structure), not the general equality. The statement remains an open expectation.
evidence_snippet: Lines 669-676: Conjecture (KKP) is stated; Prop. 674-676: conjecture holds iff the limiting Hodge structures are Hodge--Tate.

title: Vanishing orders and zero degree Turán densities

Let $k\geq 4, \ell\geq3$ be integers satisfying that $\ell<k$. Then any $k$-graph $F$ with $\pi_{\ell}(F)=0$ has an $\ell$-vanishing order.

rationale: Paper proves the ℓ=2 case (Theorem 2-vanishing) but explicitly conjectures the general ℓ≥3 statement; it is not proved here.

title: Covering complete $r$-partite hypergraphs with few monochromatic components
body_tex:

interestingness_rationale: Ryser's conjecture is a longstanding central problem in hypergraph theory, proven for r≤5 but open for r≥6; resolving it would yield major structural consequences and connects to projective planes and matching/cover theory.

title: Characterizing globally linked pairs in graphs
body_tex:
Let $G_H$ be the body-hinge graph induced by multigraph $H$
and let $d\geq 3$. Then
a pair $\{u,v\}$ is globally $d$-linked in 
$G_H$ if and only if there is a 
$\binom{d+1}{2}$-superbrick $S$ of $(\binom{d+1}{2}-1)H$
which contains the vertices of the bodies of $u$ and $v$.
plain_text: Let $G_H$ be the body-hinge graph induced by multigraph $H$ and let $d >= 3$. Then a pair $ u,v $ is globally $d$-linked in $G_H$ if and only if there is a $d+1 2 $-superbrick $S$ of $(d+1 2 -1)H$ which contains the vertices of the bodies of $u$ and $v$.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: A combinatorial characterization of globally linked pairs in body-hinge graphs extending rigidity theory results; meaningful within rigidity and graph theory though focused on a specific class of graphs.

title: Spectral Rigidity and Geometric Localization of Hopf Bifurcations in Planar Predator-Prey Systems
body_tex:
\label{conj:main}
Consider a smooth \emph{(}$C^2$ at least\emph{)} predator--prey system of the 
form~\eqref{eq:general} in the first quadrant of~$\R^2$, whose prey 
nullcline $y = g(x)$ possesses exactly two critical points in 
$(0,\infty)$: a local minimum at $x_{\min}$ and a local maximum at 
$x_{\max}$, with $0 < x_{\min} < x_{\max}$. Suppose the system admits 
exactly three coexistence equilibria. Then:
\begin{enumerate}[label=(\alph*)]
  \item Every coexistence equilibrium at which a Hopf or Bogdanov--Takens 
  bifurcation occurs has its prey coordinate in the interval 
  $(x_{\min}, x_{\max})$, i.e.\ in the region where the nullcline is 
  locally increasing \emph{(}$g'(x^*) > 0$\emph{)}.

  \item In the discrete-time \emph{(}map\emph{)} version of the system, the 
  Neimark--Sacker bifurcation occurs at equilibria satisfying 
  $g'(x^*) < 0$, i.e.\ on descending branches of the nullcline.
\end{enumerate}
This dichotomy reflects the spectral rigidity principle: at the critical 
points $g'(x) = 0$, an algebraic dependence among the Jacobian entries 
precludes the realization of the spectral conditions for bifurcation 
\emph{(}trace--zero in flows, unit determinant in maps\emph{)}, thereby confining 
bifurcations to regions determined by the monotonicity of the nullcline.
plain_text: Consider a smooth ($C^2$ at least) predator--prey system of the form in the first quadrant of $^2$, whose prey nullcline $y = g(x)$ possesses exactly two critical points in $(0,)$: a local minimum at $x_ $ and a local maximum at $x_ $, with $0 < x_ < x_ $. Suppose the system admits exactly three coexistence equilibria. Then: [label=()] Every coexistence equilibrium at which a Hopf or Bogdanov--Takens bifurcation occurs has its prey coordinate in the interval $(x_ , x_ )$, i.e.\ in the region where the nullcline is locally increasing ($g'(x^*) > 0$). In the discrete-time (map) version of the system, the Neimark--Sacker bifurcation occurs at equilibria satisfying $g'(x^*) < 0$, i.e.\ on descending branches of the nullcline. This dichotomy reflects the spectral rigidity principle: at the critical points $g'(x) = 0$, an algebraic dependence among the Jacobian entries precludes the realization of the spectral conditions for bifurcation (trace--zero in flows, unit determinant in maps), thereby confining bifurcations to regions determined by the monotonicity of the nullcline.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: A structural spectral-localization principle for Hopf/Bogdanov–Takens in planar predator–prey systems. Proven in key model families; a general smooth-nullcline proof would clarify bifurcation organization across many ecological and dynamical models.
viability_rationale: Paper proves the principle for several model families and identifies a concrete algebraic mechanism; a general C^2 planar proof seems reachable within current methods, giving moderate short-term solvability likelihood.
rationale: Authors prove several special cases (quadratic, cubic, rational) and discrete version, but state a general proof for arbitrary smooth nullclines remains open.
evidence_snippet: Lines 935–941: 'continuous-time assertion is established for quadratic, cubic, and rational nullclines... A general proof ... remains open.'
