title: Weakly turbulent dynamics on Schwarzschild-AdS black hole spacetimes
plain_text: There exists a radius of analyticity $_0>0$ such that the following holds. For every Kerr--AdS parameters $(M,a)$ satisfying the Hawking--Reall bound (with $M>0$) and every $0< 1$, there exists $=(M,a,,_0)>0$ with the following property: For any real-analytic, asymptotically Kerr--AdS initial data $(_0,K_0)$ for satisfying \[ \|(_0,K_0) - (_ M,a ,K_ M,a )\|_ <= \] for some $ >= _0$, where $\| * \|_ $ is a geometrically defined real analytic norm of the form and $(_ M,a ,K_ M,a )$ are the Kerr--AdS initial data, we have: The corresponding maximal Cauchy development $( M,g)$ of $(_0,K_0)$ with reflecting boundary conditions at $ I$ has complete conformal infinity and the black hole exterior region is $C^2$ close to the background Kerr--AdS exterior. There exist Kerr--AdS parameters $(M',a')$ with $|(M',a')-(M,a)|<$ such that, in an appropriate gauge, the black hole exterior of $( M,g)$ asymptotically settles down to the Kerr--AdS exterior with parameters $(M',a')$ at an inverse–polynomial rate: \[ \|(g(t)-g_ M',a' ,K(t)-K_ M',a' )\|_ H^s(_t) x H^ s-1 (_t) \;_s\; \,(1+t)^ -p \] for some $p>1$ and any $s>0$.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: Nonlinear stability of Kerr–AdS for analytic perturbations is a major GR problem; resolving it would have broad consequences for black hole dynamics and AdS boundary behavior, though restricted to analytic data.
viability_rationale: Nonlinear Kerr–AdS stability is a hard open GR problem; reflecting AdS boundary leads to weak turbulence (authors prove norm inflation on Schwarzschild–AdS), reducing plausibility. Significant analytical obstacles remain; resolution within five years unlikely.
rationale: This is a major open stability conjecture in general relativity stated here; the paper does not prove it and frames it as a conjecture.
evidence_snippet: There exists a radius of analyticity rho_0>0 such that ... real-analytic, asymptotically Kerr-AdS initial data ... the black hole exterior of (M,g) asymptotically settles down to the Kerr-AdS exterior with parameters (M',a') at an inverse–polynomial rate.

title: Graph Energy Maximisation for Integral Circulant Graphs of Order $n = p^2q^3$
body_tex:
\label{conj:universal}
For every pair of distinct odd primes $(p,q)$, the set
$\Dstar=\{1,p^2,pq,q^2,p^2q^2,pq^3\}$ is the unique maximiser of
$E(p^2q^3,\mathcal{D})$ over all nonempty $\mathcal{D}\subseteq
\mathrm{div}(p^2q^3)\setminus\{p^2q^3\}$.

title: On Simon's third gap conjecture for minimal surfaces in spheres
body_tex: Let $M$ be a closed surface minimally immersed in  $\mathbb{S}^N$ such that the image is not contained in any hyperplane of $\mathbb{R}^{N+1}$. If $K(s+1)\leq K\leq K(s)$ for an $s\in \mathbb{N}$, then either $K=K(s+1)$ or $K=K(s)$, and thus the immersion is one of Calabi's $2$-spheres with the dimension of the ambient space $N=2s+2$ or $N=2s$, respectively.
plain_text: Let $M$ be a closed surface minimally immersed in $S^N$ such that the image is not contained in any hyperplane of $R^ N+1 $. If $K(s+1) <= K <= K(s)$ for an $s in N$, then either $K=K(s+1)$ or $K=K(s)$, and thus the immersion is one of Calabi's $2$-spheres with the dimension of the ambient space $N=2s+2$ or $N=2s$, respectively.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: Simon’s intrinsic gap conjecture for Gaussian curvature of closed minimal surfaces is a central, longstanding rigidity problem in differential geometry with clear structural and classification consequences if resolved.
viability_rationale: Decades-old Simon conjecture largely open; only s=1,2 resolved. Recent partial progress on s=3 (this paper) helps, but broad higher-s cases remain widely open—unlikely full resolution within five years.
rationale: This is the intrinsic Simon conjecture for minimal surfaces; the paper and literature note only partial results (s=1,2) and that the third gap and full conjecture remain open.
evidence_snippet: Lines 60–63 and 76–79: state the Simon conjecture and note it has been solved only for s=1,2; the third gap remains widely open.

title: On Simon's third gap conjecture for minimal surfaces in spheres
body_tex: Let $M$ be a closed surface minimally immersed in  $\mathbb{S}^N$ such that the image is not contained in any hyperplane of $\mathbb{R}^{N+1}$. If $S(s)\leq S\leq S(s+1)$ for an $s\in\mathbb{N}$, then either $S=S(s)$ or $S=S(s+1)$, and thus the immersion is one of Calabi's $2$-spheres with the dimension of the ambient space $N=2s$ or $N=2s+2$, respectively.
plain_text: Let $M$ be a closed surface minimally immersed in $S^N$ such that the image is not contained in any hyperplane of $R^ N+1 $. label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: The extrinsic formulation of Simon’s conjecture for the squared norm S is equivalent to the intrinsic version; it is a core open rigidity problem whose resolution would strongly impact minimal-surface classification.
viability_rationale: Decades-old Simon conjecture: only s=1,2 resolved; s≥3 remains widely open. Recent partial/endpoint progress (s=3) is promising but a complete proof for all s needs substantial new ideas, so full resolution in 5 years is unlikely.
rationale: The extrinsic formulation of Simon's conjecture is stated and remains unresolved; the paper gives progress on intervals and endpoints but does not prove the full conjecture.
evidence_snippet: Lines 73–75 and 79–83: extrinsic conjecture stated; later text explains only partial results and that the third gap is still open.

title: The 1-2-3 conjecture for polygonal tilings
body_tex:
\label{conj:123}
Let $\mathcal T$ be a locally finite tiling with a finite number of polygonal prototiles. Then, there exists some partition 
\[
\cT:= \bigcupdot_{k=1}^N \left(\varLambda_k+\mathcal{P}_k\right)
\]
into translations of finitely many (not necessarily distinct) finite subgraphs, and weights 
\[
l_k: \mathcal{P}_k \to \{1,2,3\}
\]
which produce a global solution to the 1-2-3 problem.
plain_text: Let $ T$ be a locally finite tiling with a finite number of polygonal prototiles. Then, there exists some partition \[ := _ k=1 ^N (_k+P_k) \] into translations of finitely many (not necessarily distinct) finite subgraphs, and weights \[ l_k: P_k to 1,2,3 \] which produce a global solution to the 1-2-3 problem.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: A natural extension of the 1-2-3 problem to polygonal tilings; combinatorially interesting for tiling theory but relatively narrow in scope compared to major open problems.
viability_rationale: Authors solve many core cases (periodic and several aperiodic tilings), yet the conjecture asserts a very broad uniform statement across all locally finite polygonal tilings, leaving substantial nonperiodic/FLC difficulties.
rationale: The 1-2-3 conjecture for general polygonal tilings is stated; the paper proves fully periodic cases but not the full conjecture for arbitrary locally finite tilings.
evidence_snippet: Conjecture: Any locally finite polygonal tiling admits a partition and local weights giving a global 1-2-3 solution (lines 276–285). Paper proves existence for fully periodic tilings (Theorem, lines 292–294) but not the general conjecture (lines 271–286).

title: On Sets of Monochromatic Objects in Bicolored Point Sets
body_tex:
Let $n$ blue points, no three on a line, and $n$ red points, disjoint from the blue points, be given.
If the line through any two blue points contains a red point, then all $2n$ points lie on a cubic curve.
plain_text: Let $n$ blue points, no three on a line, and $n$ red points, disjoint from the blue points, be given. If the line through any two blue points contains a red point, then all $2n$ points lie on a cubic curve.

interestingness_rationale: Structural algebraic-geometric claim tying an incidence rule to containment on a cubic; proven for n=5, the general statement would strongly constrain constructions and bridge combinatorial incidence and algebraic curves.
viability_rationale: Recent structural conjecture building on Green–Tao methods; only small-n cases (here n=5) and examples on cubics known. Nontrivial global algebraic/ combinatorial obstacles remain, so moderate low near-term solvability.

title: Eigenvalue Estimates of the Hodge Laplacian Under Lower Ricci Curvature Bound
body_tex: Let $M$ be a closed Riemannian $n$-manifold with $\operatorname{Ric}_g \ge -1$, $\operatorname{diam}(M) \le D$, and $\operatorname{vol}(M) \ge v > 0$. Then the first positive eigenvalue $\lambda_{1,1}$ of the Hodge Laplacian acting on $1$-forms admits a uniform lower bound depending only on $n$, $D$, and $v$.
plain_text: Let $M$ be a closed Riemannian $n$-manifold with $Ric_g >= -1$, $diam(M) <= D$, and $vol(M) >= v > 0$. Then the first positive eigenvalue $_ 1,1 $ of the Hodge Laplacian acting on $1$-forms admits a uniform lower bound depending only on $n$, $D$, and $v$.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: A central spectral-geometry conjecture controlling the Hodge 1-form gap under Ricci lower bounds plus noncollapsing; proving it impacts eigenvalue estimates and analysis on limit spaces.
viability_rationale: Honda–Mondino conjecture is open in higher dimensions and technically challenging; known results only in low dimensions and this work avoids the full conjecture, so near-term resolution looks unlikely.
rationale: The Honda–Mondino conjecture is explicitly stated and the authors note they do not resolve it in full; they study a related, restricted setting instead.
evidence_snippet: Lines 96-100 and 105-107: 'They proposed the following conjecture. ... In the present paper, our focus is not on resolving this conjecture in full...'

title: Remarks on modules of finite projective dimension

viability_rationale: Vasconcelos's tensor-power freeness conjecture is longstanding with only partial results; it remains a hard commutative-algebra problem and unlikely to be fully settled soon.
rationale: The Vasconcelos conjecture is stated explicitly; the paper establishes special cases but does not prove the general existence of such an exponent e.

title: On Pseudo-Effectivity and Volumes of Adjoint Classes in Kähler Families with Projective Central Fiber
body_tex:
Let $f:X\to S$ be a smooth family from a K\"ahler manifold $X$ onto a smooth, connected, relatively compact curve $S$. Assume that $(X,B+\bbeta)$ is a generalized klt pair such that $(X,B)$ is log smooth over $S$ and $\bbeta =\overline \beta $ where $\beta$ is nef over $S$. Then the volume function
\[t\longmapsto {\rm vol}(K_{X_t}+B_t+\bbeta _{X_t})\]
is constant over $S$.
plain_text: Let $f:X to S$ be a smooth family from a K\"ahler manifold $X$ onto a smooth, connected, relatively compact curve $S$. Assume that $(X,B+)$ is a generalized klt pair such that $(X,B)$ is log smooth over $S$ and $ = $ where $$ is nef over $S$. Then the volume function \[t vol (K_ X_t +B_t+ _ X_t )\] is constant over $S$.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: Invariance of volumes in smooth Kähler families generalizes Siu-type invariance of plurigenera; significant for deformation theory though technically tied to MMP machinery.
viability_rationale: Volume invariance proven in treated cases (threefolds) using MMP; general Kähler-family statement in higher dimension still open and tied to deep MMP/analytic issues.
rationale: The paper proves local constancy / invariance of volumes for Kähler threefold families (and related special cases) but poses the general volume-constancy conjecture for arbitrary Kähler families as open.
evidence_snippet: Conjecture: For a smooth Kähler family with log-smooth generalized klt pair, t ↦ vol(K_{X_t}+B_t+β_{X_t}) is constant. Paper proves the threefold (and other special) cases earlier.

title: Antimagic labelling of graphs with maximum degree $Δ(G) = n - 4$
body_tex:
\label{conjalon}
    Let $G = (V,E)$ be a connected graph, with a vertex $x$ such that $d(x) = \Delta(G) = n - k$ for some $k \geq 4$. Then, if $n \geq n_0(k)$ for some $n_0(k)$, $G$ is antimagic.
plain_text: Let $G = (V,E)$ be a connected graph, with a vertex $x$ such that $d(x) = (G) = n - k$ for some $k >= 4$. Then, if $n >= n_0(k)$ for some $n_0(k)$, $G$ is antimagic.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: Alon‑style large‑degree conditional: antimagicness for graphs with a vertex of degree n−k when n≥n0(k). Meaningful for large‑Δ cases but narrower than the global antimagic conjecture.
viability_rationale: Partial results (Δ≥n−2, Δ=n−3; here Δ=n−4 with m≥7n) exist but progress has been slow. Extending to all fixed k≥4 is broad and technical—modest chance of full resolution within five years.
rationale: The Alon-style conjecture for Δ(G)=n−k with k≥4 is stated; the paper proves a related k=4 result only under an additional m≥7n hypothesis, not the exact n≥n0(k) statement.
evidence_snippet: “Let G ... d(x)=Δ(G)=n−k for some k≥4. Then, if n≥n_0(k) for some n_0(k), G is antimagic.” (conjecture stated; paper proves k=4 with extra m≥7n)

title: Computing the Skyscraper Invariant
body_tex:
\label{obs:factor_dimension}
Modules coming from the first persistent homology group of a density-scale bifiltration (\autoref{subsec:bifiltr}) of a generic pointset in $\bbR^2$ do not contain indecomposable uniquely generated submodules of dimension larger than 1. In particular, all semistable factors of the Harder-Narasimhan filtration are interval modules.
plain_text: Modules coming from the first persistent homology group of a density-scale bifiltration (subsec:bifiltr) of a generic pointset in $^2$ do not contain indecomposable uniquely generated submodules of dimension larger than 1. In particular, all semistable factors of the Harder-Narasimhan filtration are interval modules.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: Specialist conjecture in multiparameter TDA: if true it simplifies HN factors to intervals for common density-scale bifiltrations, aiding computation and interpretation, but its impact is mostly within the subfield.

title: CayleyPy-4: AI-Holography. Towards analogs of holographic string dualities for AI tasks
body_tex:
[Determinant. Spanning trees]\label{conj:det2}
    Logarithm of the number of spanning trees (which is related to determinant of XXX Hamiltonian by Kirchhoff's theorem) normalized by the graph size -- 
    behaves as $log(n)$ at leading order.
    This corresponds to central charge 1 from the CFT perspective, confirming the free boson as the dual model. 
    
    The subleading term (independent of $n$  -- "universal") corresponds to twice the area of the dual polygon $log(2\sum_{i<j}(k_i/n)(k_j/n)) $. Which should be thought as logarithm of "renormalized" number of spanning trees. 
    
    For $S_n/(S_d \times S_{n-d})$, it is expected: 
    
$\frac{1}{\binom{n}{d}-1} \log \tau_n = \log(n) + \log(2a(1-a)) - \frac{1 + 2a(1-a)}{4a(1-a)} \frac{1}{n} + O(n^{-2})$, where $a = d/n$

    For $S_n$ (no quotients), it is expected: 

    $\frac{1}{n!-1} \log \tau_n = \log(n-1) - \frac{1}{2(n-1)}
    - \frac{3}{4(n-1)^2} - \frac{15}{6(n-1)^3} -  ... \frac{(2m-1)!!}{2m (n-1)^{m} } - ...
    +   O(exp{(-n}))$.
    The constant terms disappears
    since $0=log(1) = log(2\times 1/2)$,
    here $1/2$ is triangle area,
    taken twice gives 1, logarithm gives zero.
plain_text: [Determinant. Spanning trees] Logarithm of the number of spanning trees (which is related to determinant of XXX Hamiltonian by Kirchhoff's theorem) normalized by the graph size -- behaves as $log(n)$ at leading order. This corresponds to central charge 1 from the CFT perspective, confirming the free boson as the dual model. The subleading term (independent of $n$ -- "universal") corresponds to twice the area of the dual polygon $log(2_ i<j (k_i/n)(k_j/n)) $. Which should be thought as logarithm of "renormalized" number of spanning trees. For $S_n/(S_d x S_ n-d )$, it is expected: $1 n d -1 _n = (n) + (2a(1-a)) - 1 + 2a(1-a) 4a(1-a) 1 n + O(n^ -2 )$, where $a = d/n$ For $S_n$ (no quotients), it is expected: $1 n!-1 _n = (n-1) - 1 2(n-1) - 3 4(n-1)^2 - 15 6(n-1)^3 - ... (2m-1)!! 2m (n-1)^ m - ... + O(exp (-n ))$. The constant terms disappears since $0=log(1) = log(2 x 1/2)$, here $1/2$ is triangle area, taken twice gives 1, logarithm gives zero.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: Asymptotics for log spanning-tree counts linking to central charge and CFT data is a substantial structural claim tying combinatorics, spectral determinants and conformal theory; significant within mathematical physics.
viability_rationale: Leading-order log(n) behaviour for log(#spanning trees) is plausible from Gaussian/spectral heuristics; full detailed expansion and universal constants require delicate control and are moderately hard.
rationale: The asymptotics for log number of spanning trees (leading log n and universal subleading terms) are conjectured; the paper notes the permutohedron spanning-tree count is an open problem in the literature.

title: Asymptotically optimal lower bounds on weak saturation numbers for hypergraphs

    \[
        \gamma_{s,H} \ge \frac{\delta}{\binom{r}{s-1}} - \frac{1}{\binom{k}{s - 1}}.
    \]

[ii)] The action of $T_(_p)$ on $ Sh _ K_1 (, )_E$ extends to $_ K_1, S $ and the morphism $_ K_1, S to _ K_0 $ of (i) identifies $_ K_0 $ with the scheme quotient $T_(_p) _ K_1, S $. viability_rationale: Authors prove PEL/Iwahori cases; conjecture depends on the divisor conjecture and convexity. Active progress for Hodge/abelian-type and tame cases likely, but full generality (wild ramification, all parahorics) remains hard.
rationale: The global existence conjecture is posed in full generality (Conjecture \ref{globconj}) and the paper only proves special PEL/Iwahori cases, so the full statement remains open.

title: Towards direct $L^2$-bounds for maximal partial sums of Walsh--Fourier series: The case of dyadic partial sums
body_tex:
\label{thm:uniform_bound} Let $D$ denote the collection of measurable functions $\Phi:[0,1]\to 2^{\mathbb{N}}$ such that $\Phi\in\mathcal{D}_M$ and $\Phi\leq 2^M$ for some $M\in\mathbb{N}$.  For $S_\Phi$ defined as in (\ref{eq:sphi}) one has 
$\sup_{\Phi\in D}\|S_\Phi\|_{L^2\to L^2}\leq 1+\frac{\sqrt{2}}{2}$.

label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: Concrete sharp operator-norm bound in Walsh analysis whose proof yields direct L^2 a.e. convergence for dyadic partial sums; a useful technical advance within harmonic analysis/operator theory.
viability_rationale: Finite-dimensional matrix reduction, numerical evidence and an outlined approach give a concrete route; problem is specialized (not a decades-old grand conjecture) but requires nontrivial matrix/operator analysis to complete.
rationale: The author frames the uniform operator-norm bound over D as a conjecture and no proof is provided in the paper; it's posed as an open target.
evidence_snippet: Lines 293-296: "Conjecture: Let D denote... For S_Φ defined as in (...) one has sup_{Φ∈D} ||S_Φ||_{L^2→L^2} ≤ 1+√2/2."

viability_rationale: Paper gives partial dyadic progress and an outlined dilation approach, but no proof; identifying optimal truncations is termed intractable. Plausible frontier problem with concrete ideas, but significant technical gaps remain.
rationale: The general uniform bound over bounded measurable Φ is posed as a conjecture in the paper and is not proved; only an outline/approach is discussed.

title: Random Polyhedral Cones: Distributional Results via Gale Duality
body_tex: The sequence of measures $(\mu_d)_{d\in \N}$ with   $\mu_d(A) := 2^d d \cdot \P[\alpha_d \in A]$, $A\subseteq (0,\infty)$, converges weakly to a non-trivial limit.
plain_text: The sequence of measures $(_d)_ d in $ with $_d(A) := 2^d d * $, $A subseteq (0,)$, converges weakly to a non-trivial limit.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: Nontrivial high-dimensional weak limit describing rare "spikes" of the solid angle; ties directly to moment asymptotics and would clarify rare-event geometry of random polyhedral cones in geometric probability.
viability_rationale: Moment asymptotics and heuristics support the 2^d d scaling, but proving weak convergence requires delicate extreme-value/Poisson-type analysis of rare geometric configurations—nontrivial yet modestly attainable within five years.
rationale: Paper explicitly poses this as an open conjecture on weak limits of rescaled measures and does not prove it; no resolution provided in the text.
evidence_snippet: The sequence of measures (μ_d) with μ_d(A):=2^d d · P[α_d∈A] converges weakly to a non-trivial limit. (Conjecture stated, lines 1128–1130)

title: Random Polyhedral Cones: Distributional Results via Gale Duality
body_tex: Fix $k\ge 2$. The conditional distribution of $\alpha(\cW_{d+k,d})$, given that $\cW_{d+k,d}\cap \bS^{d-1}$ is a spherical simplex, converges weakly (as $d\to\infty$) to a limit law not concentrated at $0$.
plain_text: Fix $k >= 2$. The conditional distribution of $(_ d+k,d )$, given that $_ d+k,d cap ^ d-1 $ is a spherical simplex, converges weakly (as $d to $) to a limit law not concentrated at $0$.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: Predicts a nondegenerate limiting conditional law for the solid angle given the spherical-simplex event; meaningful for the proposed sparse-spike mechanism but narrower in scope within high-dimensional geometric probability.
viability_rationale: Paper gives a concrete mechanism and related asymptotic tools (Gale duality, moments); for fixed k this seems reachable via extreme-value/rare-event analysis, but conditioning on a rare event introduces nontrivial technical obstacles.
rationale: Paper formulates this conditional weak‑limit statement as a conjecture for fixed k and large d and does not prove it; remains an open target in the article.
evidence_snippet: Fix k≥2. The conditional distribution of α(W_{d+k,d}), given W_{d+k,d}∩S^{d-1} is a spherical simplex, converges weakly (as d→∞) to a limit law not concentrated at 0. (Conjecture, lines 1244–1246)

title: Symmetries and Higher-Form Connections in Derived Differential Geometry

Then: myenumerate The stacks $_v(M, ^2\, _ (1))/(M)$ and $_v(M, _ CS (K))/(M)$ have (possibly quasi-coherent) deformation theory. A lift of $P_K:M K$ to $ _ CS (K)$ exists if and only if a lift to $ (K)$ exists. The $L_$-algebra $T(* /\, (_v(M, _ CS (K))/(M)))$ has a canonical map to the Lie algebra $TM$, and a $C^(M)$-linear splitting of this map (as dg $C^(M)$-modules) is the same as a geometric string structure on $P_K$ together with a symmetric 2-tensor $ in (M, ^2\, T^*M)$. myenumerate
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: Links geometric string structures, deformation theory of stacks, and L_infty-splittings encoding metric data; consequential for mathematical formulations of heterotic supergravity and for understanding string connections.
viability_rationale: Motivated by examples and existing literature, parts appear reachable via established higher-geometric and deformation-theory techniques; the three-part statement is plausible but technically involved.
rationale: The three-part statement (deformation theory, equivalence of lifts, description of L_\infty splitting) is presented as Conjecture~\ref{conj:hetSuGra} and is not proved in the paper.
evidence_snippet: Conjecture~\ref{conj:hetSuGra}: The stacks Hom_v(...)/cDiff(M) have deformation theory; lift to BString_CS(K) iff lift to BString(K); description of L_\infty-algebra splitting.

title: Arithmetic exceptionality of Latt\`{e}s maps
body_tex:
\label{C:reform}
    For any positive integer $k$ such that $6\nmid k$, the $k$-th Latt\`es map attached to an elliptic curve $E/\mathbb{Q}$ is arithmetically exceptional if and only if $E$ possesses no $k$-torsion point whose $x$-coordinate is rational.
plain_text: For any positive integer $k$ such that $6 k$, the $k$-th Latt\`es map attached to an elliptic curve $E/Q$ is arithmetically exceptional if and only if $E$ possesses no $k$-torsion point whose $x$-coordinate is rational.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: A precise arithmetic-dynamical criterion linking Lattès maps to rational torsion on elliptic curves; proven in many CM cases, this conjecture bridges dynamics and arithmetic and has substantial interest.
viability_rationale: CM cases largely resolved and counterexamples clarified the 6-divisibility restriction; partial non-CM progress via Galois representations exists but remaining global Galois subtleties make a full resolution plausible yet uncertain within five years.
rationale: This reformulation (excluding 6|k) is explicitly posed as a conjecture; the paper proves it for many CM cases but not for non-CM curves, so the full statement remains open.
evidence_snippet: Lines 1273–1276: "Based on our results ... we reformulate Odabaş's conjecture: For k with 6∤k, L_k is arithmetically exceptional iff no k-torsion point has rational x-coordinate."

title: A cube dismantling problem related to bootstrap percolation
body_tex: $s_3(n)\ge n^2+6n-4$ for all $n$.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: Specific combinatorial lower bound for 3D solution projection area; leading term is proved so conjecture refines lower-order terms — a narrow extremal-combinatorics question tied to this paper.
viability_rationale: Leading term proven and many partial/constructive results (small n counts, asymptotic constructions); combinatorial nature suggests a concrete finite-gap attack, moderate chance within 5 years.
rationale: Paper states the inequality as an explicit conjecture and only proves the leading term (Proposition) and asymptotic results, not the full inequality for all n.
evidence_snippet: Lines 676–681 state the conjecture; Proposition (686–689) only gives the correct leading term, not a full proof.

title: CayleyPy-4: AI-Holography. Towards analogs of holographic string dualities for AI tasks

interestingness_rationale: Proposes a concrete AdS/CFT-like correspondence: graph quantum theory → free boson CFT with radius set by polygon area. Broad conceptual bridge with strong consequences if rigorously established.
viability_rationale: Rigorous emergence of a specific CFT (free boson with geometric worldsheet) from these discrete models is deep and ambitious; unlikely to be fully settled within five years absent breakthrough methods.

title: On Worst-Case Optimal Polynomial Intersection
plain_text: bdir ] The Massey scheme defined with an MDS code $C$ over a prime field $F_p$ is one-bit local leakage resilient for any positive rate.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: Establishes universal one-bit local leakage resilience for Massey schemes built from MDS codes over prime fields—significant for secret-sharing and leakage-resilient cryptography; requires nontrivial coding-theoretic arguments.
viability_rationale: Broad, strong claim across all positive rates; active area with partial progress but no explicit closing route here. Likely requires new coding/cryptographic techniques, so resolution within five years is possible but not probable.
rationale: The paper cites this conjecture and develops related leakage-resilience results for certain rate thresholds (e.g. n/m ≥ 0.6225, 0.7496) but does not claim to prove one-bit local leakage resilience for every positive rate.
evidence_snippet: Conjecture: The Massey scheme defined with an MDS code C over a prime field F_p is one-bit local leakage resilient for any positive rate.

title: Duflo-Serganova functors and Brundan-Goodwin's parabolic inductions
body_tex:
\label{conj:BGN}
Let $\mathfrak g=\mathfrak{gl}(n|n)$, let $\mathfrak b$ be a Borel subalgebra, and let $\alpha$ be a
$\mathfrak b$-simple odd root. Then for $\lambda\in\Lambda$ one has
\[
\operatorname{DS}_{e_\alpha}\bigl(M^{\mathfrak b}(\lambda)\bigr)\ \cong\
\begin{cases}
M^{\mathfrak b_{e_\alpha}}\bigl(\operatorname{pr}_\alpha(\lambda)\bigr)\ \oplus\
\Pi\,M^{\mathfrak b_{e_\alpha}}\bigl(\operatorname{pr}_\alpha(\lambda)\bigr),
& \text{if }(\lambda,\alpha)=0,\\[2mm]
0, & \text{if }(\lambda,\alpha)\neq 0.
\end{cases}
\]
Here $\operatorname{pr}_\alpha$ is the natural projection on weight lattices associated with $\alpha$, defined
in \S6.
plain_text: Let $ g=gl(n|n)$, let $ b$ be a Borel subalgebra, and let $$ be a $ b$-simple odd root. Then for $ in $ one has \[ DS_ e_ (M^ b ())\ \ cases M^ b_ e_ (pr_())\ \ \,M^ b_ e_ (pr_()), & if (,)=0, [2mm] 0, & if (,) != 0. cases \] Here $pr_$ is the natural projection on weight lattices associated with $$, defined in 6.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: A structural prediction about DS images of b-Verma modules in gl(n|n); resolving it would clarify Duflo–Serganova behavior, category O structure, and links to Brundan–Goodwin parabolic induction and Whittaker functors.
viability_rationale: Conjecture verified for hypercube Borels and gl(2|2) with substantial partial results in the paper; remaining generalization seems technical and plausible but nontrivial.
rationale: The paper states this as a conjecture and only proves it in special settings (hypercube Borel / specific e_α) and checks gl(2|2); the full general statement for all Borels remains unresolved here.
evidence_snippet: “In §5 we show ... in a setting which includes the case where b is a hypercube Borel and e_α=e_{1,n+1}, we prove the following conjecture.”; verified for gl(2|2) in §6.

title: A Geometric Approach to the Links-Quivers Correspondence I: Rational Tangles
body_tex:
\label{LQCC}
   Given a link $L$ with $|L|$ components, if we set 
   \[
   P(L)=\sum_{j\geq 0}\left(\prod_{i=1}^j(1-q^{2i})\right)^{|L|-2}P^{\bigwedge^j}_L(q,a)x^j,
   \]
   then there is a symmetric quiver $Q_L$ such that $P(L)$ and $P_{Q_L}(\overline{x})$ are equal after a change of variables.
plain_text: Given a link $L$ with $|L|$ components, if we set \[ P(L)=_ j >= 0 (_ i=1 ^j(1-q^ 2i ))^ |L|-2 P^ ^j _L(q,a)x^j, \] then there is a symmetric quiver $Q_L$ such that $P(L)$ and $P_ Q_L (x)$ are equal after a change of variables.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: A structural bridge between colored HOMFLY-PT link invariants and symmetric quiver/DT generating functions; proving it broadly would deeply connect knot theory with enumerative/quiver geometry and impact multiple active research directions.
viability_rationale: Proven for rational tangles/links (SW21 and this work); significant local geometric progress exists, but the full Links–Quivers statement for arbitrary links is broad and lacks a clear global reduction—unlikely in five years.
rationale: The Links–Quivers Correspondence is stated as a general conjecture for all links; only special cases (rational links) have been proved (Stošić–Wedrich), so the full statement remains open.

The Links-Quivers Correspondence could be phrased as follows. ... In 
\cite{SW21}, they prove by induction that P'(τ_{u/v}) can be written ... Stošić and Wedrich proved the conjecture for rational links in \cite{SW21}.

title: Burning rooted graph products
body_tex:
\label{conj: tooth greedy}
If $n \le m$, then
\[
b(C_{n,m}) = \min\left\{T_\gr^{(S)} : S = 1,2,\ldots,n\right\}.
\]
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: Conjectured optimality of a specific greedy algorithm for combs in the tooth-dominant regime is a useful algorithmic/local claim but is a narrow, technically focused statement.
viability_rationale: Specific optimality-of-greedy claim for comb graphs in a narrow regime; authors have strong structural analysis and examples. Likely resolvable (proof or counterexample) in next five years.
rationale: This paper explicitly poses optimality of the greedy algorithm for combs (n ≤ m) as a conjecture and does not prove it; it remains an open specific statement.

title: Burning rooted graph products
body_tex: If $T$ is a tree, then $b(T) \le \lceil \sqrt n \rceil$.
plain_text: If $T$ is a tree, then $b(T) <= n $.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: The tree-specialization suffices to imply BNC and focuses a hard global conjecture to a natural, structured class; meaningful to specialists but narrower than the full conjecture.
viability_rationale: Long-standing Burning Number Conjecture for trees remains open despite many resolved special classes (paths, caterpillars, spiders); current paper handles combs only and gives no clear general breakthrough, so low 5-year solvability.
rationale: The tree-version is posed in the paper as a sufficient (still-open) conjecture to imply BNC; only special classes of trees are resolved, not the general tree case.
evidence_snippet: As such, the BNC can reduce to showing b(T) ≤ ⌈√n⌉ for T any spanning tree. Hence, a sufficient conjecture to establish the BNC is: If T is a tree, then b(T) ≤ ⌈√n⌉.

title: Surface topology and incidence theorems over division rings
body_tex: Let $\mathcal T$ be a simple, $(n+1)$-degenerate tiling of a surface of positive genus, and let $\D$ be a division ring. Then the incidence theorem corresponding to $\mathcal T$ holds in $\DP^n$ if and only if $\D$ is commutative.
plain_text: Let $ T$ be a simple, $(n+1)$-degenerate tiling of a surface of positive genus, and let $$ be a division ring. Then the incidence theorem corresponding to $ T$ holds in $^n$ if and only if $$ is commutative.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: Links surface topology of tilings to algebraic commutativity of division rings, giving a deep geometric–algebraic dichotomy; significant within incidence geometry and noncommutative projective theory.
viability_rationale: Authors give constructive counterexamples and an inductive 3-degenerate method, plus genus-dependent degeneracy bounds. Extension to all (n+1)-degenerate tilings seems plausible but nontrivial, so moderate near-term solvability likelihood.
rationale: The author explicitly states this as a conjecture (stronger version of a proved result) about degenerate tilings and division rings; it is not proved in the paper.
evidence_snippet: Conjecture: Let 𝒯 be a simple, (n+1)-degenerate tiling of a surface of positive genus, and let 𝔻 be a division ring. Then the incidence theorem ... holds in ℙ𝔻^n iff 𝔻 is commutative.

title: Surface topology and incidence theorems over division rings
body_tex:
For any $g > 0$, there exists a constant $n_g$ such that, for any simple tiling $\mathcal T$ of a genus $g$ surface, the corresponding  incidence theorem over a division ring $\D$ is equivalent to commutativity of $\D$
in any dimension $n \geq n_g$. In particular, one has $n_1 = 3$, so that any theorem in dimension $n \geq 3$ corresponding to a tiling of the torus holds over a division ring $\D$ if and only if $\D$ is commutative.
plain_text: For any $g > 0$, there exists a constant $n_g$ such that, for any simple tiling $ T$ of a genus $g$ surface, the corresponding incidence theorem over a division ring $$ is equivalent to commutativity of $$ in any dimension $n >= n_g$. In particular, one has $n_1 = 3$, so that any theorem in dimension $n >= 3$ corresponding to a tiling of the torus holds over a division ring $$ if and only if $$ is commutative.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: A uniform genus-version that would tie genus to minimal ambient dimension for commutativity equivalence; broader and more structural than single examples but still specialized to incidence/topology interaction.
viability_rationale: Paper provides partial results, reductions to k-degeneracy and explicit torus evidence (n1=3); plausible but needs nontrivial combinatorial/topological extensions for all genera, so medium near-term solvability.
rationale: Presented as a conjectural consequence (existence of n_g with n_1 = 3) of the previous conjecture; the paper does not prove this general statement.
evidence_snippet: Conjecture: For any g>0, there exists a constant n_g such that, for any simple tiling 𝒯 of a genus g surface, the corresponding incidence theorem over a division ring 𝔻 is equivalent to commutativity of 𝔻 in any dimension n ≥ n_g. In particular, n_1 = 3.

title: The interplay of selection and dormancy in a Moran model can lead to coexistence of types

title: Improved, sublinear projective Schwartz-Zippel and (sub)quadratic dimension growth bounds in arbitrary codimension
body_tex:
\label{SLSZ}
        A projective variety of degree $d$ and pure dimension $k$ in $\PP_\QQ^n$ contains no more than $cd^\frac{n}{n+1}B^{k+1}$ points of height $\leq B$ for some constant $c$ depending only on $n$ and $k$.
plain_text: A projective variety of degree $d$ and pure dimension $k$ in $_^n$ contains no more than $cd^n n+1 B^ k+1 $ points of height $ <= B$ for some constant $c$ depending only on $n$ and $k$.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: A sublinear degree exponent in projective Schwartz–Zippel would be optimal (matching lower bounds) and significantly improve rational-point counting/dimension-growth results, making it a substantial advance in arithmetic geometry/counting.
viability_rationale: Significant partial progress: proved for unions of linear varieties, n=2 fully, n=3 up to log factors, and several low-dim cases. Full arbitrary n,k likely needs new ideas, so moderate near-term solvability.
rationale: The authors formulate the sublinear projective Schwartz–Zippel as a conjecture and prove various special cases and near-results, but do not establish the full conjecture here.
evidence_snippet: “Conjecture (Sublinear projective Schwartz-Zippel): A projective variety ... contains no more than c d^{n/(n+1)} B^{k+1} points ...” followed by proofs only in special cases (lines 448–452).

title: Variants of Merge-Width and Applications
body_tex:
\label{conj:univ}
Every class of bounded merge-width has bounded universal merge-width.
plain_text: Every class of bounded merge-width has bounded universal merge-width.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: Equivalence of bounded merge-width and universal merge-width would unify variants and boost structural and algorithmic consequences for graph classes; known in key subclasses but nontrivial in full generality.
viability_rationale: Conjecture holds for bounded expansion, bounded twin-width, and is stable under FO transductions per the paper; promising partial progress but general case requires new ideas, so moderate near-term solvability chance.
rationale: The paper explicitly conjectures equivalence (bounded merge-width implies bounded universal merge-width) and only verifies it in special cases; the general statement is not proved here.
evidence_snippet: “We conjecture the following. Every class of bounded merge-width has bounded universal merge-width.” (lines 889–893), with only corroborating special-case results given afterwards.

interestingness_rationale: A twisted Malle-type asymptotic for counting fibers of Galois-surjection maps; resolving it would substantially advance arithmetic statistics and field-counting techniques, linking group actions and analytic multiple-Dirichlet-series methods.
viability_rationale: Deep analytic/Galois obstacles (Malle-type, subconvexity, class group torsion); only conditional and special-case progress so full twisted counting conjecture unlikely within five years.
rationale: The paper formulates the twisted counting conjecture generally but only proves it in specific cases (e.g. nilpotent N under hypotheses). The full general twisted conjecture remains unresolved.

label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: Asserting that optimal hypercontractive constants depend only on the smallest atom mass would markedly simplify classification for biased Bernoulli measures; significant within hypercontractivity though confined to this setting.
viability_rationale: Numerical evidence and Wolff's theorem suggest two-point extremizers; plausible symmetrization/convexity routes exist, but a uniform proof for all (p,q) and finite measures remains nontrivial.
rationale: The authors formulate this equality conjecture for biased measures based on simulations; no proof or disproof is provided in the paper.
evidence_snippet: Inspired by the simulations ... we formulate the following conjecture: Let mu be a probability measure on a finite set with least atom mass lambda in (0,1/2). Then for all 1<p<q<infty, sigma_{p,q}(mu)=sigma_{p,q}(lambda).

title: Serre depth and local cohomology
body_tex:
\label{ConjG}
		Let $(R,\m)$ be a Noetherian local ring or a standard graded $K$-algebra. Let $I\subset R$ be an ideal, which we assume is homogeneous if $R$ is a $K$-algebra. Then
		$$
		S_r\text{-}\!\depth(R/I^{k+1})\ =\ S_r\text{-}\!\depth(R/I^k),
		$$
		for all $r\ge1$ and all $k\gg0$.
plain_text: Let $(R,)$ be a Noetherian local ring or a standard graded $K$-algebra. Let $I subset R$ be an ideal, which we assume is homogeneous if $R$ is a $K$-algebra. Then $$ S_r-\!(R/I^ k+1 )\ =\ S_r-\!(R/I^k), $$ for all $r1$ and all $k0$.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: Natural broad generalization of Brodmann-type stability to Serre depths; proven in key cases (monomial, low dimension) but the full asymptotic behaviour for arbitrary ideals has clear structural consequences in commutative algebra.
viability_rationale: Significant partial results (monomial case, dim≤3, many structural reductions) and new methods (Presburger arithmetic) give a realistic pathway; full generality still nontrivial but plausibly reachable within 5 years.
rationale: The paper states Conjecture G for arbitrary Noetherian local or standard graded rings but only proves several special cases (monomial ideals, dim ≤3, etc.), leaving the general statement unresolved.
evidence_snippet: Lines 258-266: 'we expect: Conjecture ... S_r-depth(R/I^{k+1}) = S_r-depth(R/I^k) for all r≥1 and all k≫0.' Theorem F (lines 246-251) proves the monomial polynomial case.

title: Spectrum, Tjurina spectrum, and Hertling conjecture for singularities of modality $\leq 3$
plain_text: Suppose $f$ is itself a $$-max form. Let $_1,...,_$ be exponents of Tjurina spectrum. Then the following inequality holds: align* 1 _ i = 1 ^ (_i- )^2 <= _-_1 12 ,GHCTS align* where $ $ is the average of $_1,...,_$ i.e. $ = 1 _ j=1 ^ _j$.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: A natural but narrower analogue of Hertling for the newer Tjurina spectrum; meaningful within studies of Hodge ideals and singularity invariants, but more specialized than the original conjecture.
viability_rationale: Paper proves the conjecture for modality ≤3 and recent work resolved related maximal-exponent issues, but the full general case across all isolated hypersurface singularities remains broad and likely needs new ideas.
rationale: The Generalized Hertling Conjecture for Tjurina Spectrum is posed in general; the paper proves it only for modality ≤3, leaving the general conjecture open.
evidence_snippet: Lines 251-256: 'Suppose f is a τ-max form... (GHCTS) inequality.' Line 272: 'Conjecture GHCTS holds for singularities of modality ≤3.'

title: The realization problem of essential surfaces in knot exteriors
body_tex:
\label{strong}
For any even integer $b \ge 6$, the triple $(0, b, 1)$ is not realizable.
plain_text: For any even integer $b >= 6$, the triple $(0, b, 1)$ is not realizable.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: A strong form of the cabling/realization obstruction for planar essential surfaces; connects to the Cabling Conjecture and has substantial consequences in 3-manifold and knot theory, making it a central open problem.
viability_rationale: Stronger than the celebrated Cabling Conjecture; only small cases (b=1,2,4) are known. No concrete new route presented; long-standing Dehn surgery problem unlikely to be resolved within five years.
rationale: The Strong Cabling Conjecture is stated as an open conjecture in the introduction; the paper does not prove or refute it and instead proves other existence results.
evidence_snippet: Conjecture [Strong Cabling]: 'For any even integer b ≥ 6, the triple (0, b, 1) is not realizable.' (lines 197–201)

title: Algebraic representatives of the ratios $ζ(2n+1)/π^{2n}$ and $β(2n)/π^{2n-1}$

viability_rationale: Explicit closed formulas and structural lemmas in the paper give concrete analytic handles; remaining gap seems technical and likely resolvable by estimating zeros from the Eulerian-number expressions.
rationale: The paper states this limit as a conjecture (no proof) after proving related but different limits; the conjecture is presented without resolution in the text.

title: Pruning distance of upset-decomposable persistence modules
body_tex:
\label{conjecture}
Let~$M$ and~$N$ be pfd modules with~$r = \operatorname{supdim} M<\infty$.
Then
\[
d_P(M,N)\leq d_I(M,N)\leq 2r\,d_P(M,N).
\]
plain_text: Let $M$ and $N$ be pfd modules with $r = supdim M<$. Then \[ d_P(M,N) <= d_I(M,N) <= 2r\,d_P(M,N). \]
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: Asserts Lipschitz equivalence between pruning and interleaving distances—central to stability and metric comparisons in persistence theory, with clear structural and practical consequences if resolved.
viability_rationale: Active area with partial results (including this paper's sharp bound for upset-decomposable modules); full general Lipschitz equivalence to interleaving distance remains nontrivial but plausibly resolved in coming years.
rationale: The conjecture (d_P ≤ d_I ≤ 2r d_P for pfd modules) is stated and discussed as open; this paper proves related results for upset-decomposable modules but not the full general statement.
evidence_snippet: Lines 438–445 and 447–452: Conjecture states d_P(M,N) ≤ d_I(M,N) ≤ 2r d_P(M,N). Paper notes left inequality was partially verified elsewhere and extension remains open; authors prove results for upset-decomposable modules only.

title: Half-periodicity of Zamolodchikov periodic cluster algebras
body_tex:
Let $(\Gamma, \Delta)$ be an admissible Dynkin biagram with $r$ vertices.
    The number of $\Gamma$-mutations in one period is the total number of roots in the root systems associated to each $\Gamma$-component, $h_{\Gamma}\cdot r$.
    Similarly, the number of $\Delta$-mutations in one period is the total number of roots in the root systems associated to each $\Delta$-component, $h_{\Delta}\cdot r$.
plain_text: Let $(, )$ be an admissible Dynkin biagram with $r$ vertices. The number of $$-mutations in one period is the total number of roots in the root systems associated to each $$-component, $h_ * r$. Similarly, the number of $$-mutations in one period is the total number of roots in the root systems associated to each $$-component, $h_ * r$.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: Combinatorial conjecture linking colored mutation counts to root numbers/Coxeter data in Zamolodchikov-periodic cluster algebras; a neat structural pattern but narrowly scoped within cluster combinatorics.
viability_rationale: Substantial progress in the paper (half-periodicity proved and several Dynkin cases verified); the conjecture is combinatorial/finite-case in nature, so completion appears likely soon.
rationale: The conjecture is stated and is only verified in the paper under a certain global ordering/certain cases; the full general statement is not proved here.
evidence_snippet: Conjecture (1151–1156) asserts counts of Γ-/Δ-mutations equal h_Γ·r and h_Δ·r. Corollary (1158–1164) verifies it under a specific global ordering only.

title: Monotonicity of depth constants in general preferential attachment trees
body_tex:
\label{conj:GRD-monotone}
Let $f,g:\N_0\to\R_{>0}$ be nondecreasing and assume $f\succeq_{\mathrm{GR}} g$.
Then for all $n\ge 1$,
\[
  \E_f[D_n] \;\le\; \E_g[D_n], \text{ and }  \E_f[H_n] \;\le\; \E_g[H_n].
\]
plain_text: Let $f,g:_0 to _ >0 $ be nondecreasing and assume $f_ GR g$. Then for all $n >= 1$, \[ _f[D_n] \; <= \; _g[D_n], and _f[H_n] \; <= \; _g[H_n]. \]
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: Finite-n monotonicity strengthening of proven asymptotic monotonicity for preferential-attachment trees; a meaningful probabilistic-combinatorics conjecture with clear content but limited cross-field reach.
viability_rationale: Authors proved asymptotic monotonicity and developed embedding tools; finite-n monotonicity likely approachable via coupling/ordering arguments though delicate, making near-term resolution plausible.
rationale: The paper states this finite-n monotonicity conjecture and only proves asymptotic versions (limits); the exact finite-n inequalities remain unresolved here.
evidence_snippet: Lines 415–421: Conjecture asserts for all n≥1 E_f[D_n]≤E_g[D_n], E_f[H_n]≤E_g[H_n]. Lines 423–427: authors prove asymptotic forms (limits) only.

title: Note on the Coefficient Conjecture of Clunie and Sheil-Small on the univalent harmonic mapping
plain_text: All functions $f=h+g in S_ H $, satisfy the inequalities equation |a_n| <= 2(1+a)n^2+3(1-a)n+(1+a) 6 , \ \ |b_n| <= 2(1+a)n^2+3(a-1)n+(1+a) 6 equation for all $n2$, where $-1<a<1$.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: Concrete improvement of the Clunie–Sheil‑Small coefficient conjecture for class S_H; significant to univalent harmonic mapping theory but restricted to a specialist area and a technical coefficient problem.
viability_rationale: Specialized coefficient bound after original conjecture was counterexampled; authors provide concrete models and partial bounds—tractable for experts but nontrivial, so moderate near-term solvability likelihood.
rationale: The authors present this as an "improved conjecture" (Con) for all f in S_H but do not prove it in the paper; it is proposed without a completed proof.
evidence_snippet: "We proposed the improved conjecture for all functions... in the following form" followed by the Con (lines 251–258).

title: Research Report: some constructions in Polyhedral Symplectic Topology suggested by AI
body_tex:
Let $\Gamma_{v,\delta} \subset (S^3,\xi_{\mathrm{std}})$ be the Legendrian link obtained by intersecting a locally smoothed valence--$4$ vertex with a sufficiently small sphere. Then the following hold:

\smallskip

\noindent
\textup{(i)} If the polyhedral Maslov index satisfies 
\[
\mu(K,v)=0,
\]
then 
\[
\mathrm{rot}(\Gamma_v^\varepsilon)=0
\quad\text{and}\quad
tb(\Gamma_v^\varepsilon)=-1.
\]
In particular, $\Gamma_v^\varepsilon$ is Legendrian isotopic to the standard Legendrian unknot.

\smallskip

\noindent
\textup{(ii)} If the polyhedral Maslov index satisfies 
\[
\mu(K,v)\not=0,
\]
then 
\[
\mathrm{rot}(\Gamma_v^\varepsilon)\neq 0,
\]
and therefore $\Gamma_v^\varepsilon$ cannot bound a Lagrangian disk inside $B_\varepsilon(v)$. In particular, the vertex is not locally Lagrangian smoothable.
plain_text: Let $_ v, subset (S^3,_ std )$ be the Legendrian link obtained by intersecting a locally smoothed valence--$4$ vertex with a sufficiently small sphere. Then the following hold: (i) If the polyhedral Maslov index satisfies \[ (K,v)=0, \] then \[ rot(_v^)=0 tb(_v^)=-1. \] In particular, $_v^$ is Legendrian isotopic to the standard Legendrian unknot. (ii) If the polyhedral Maslov index satisfies \[ (K,v)=0, \] then \[ rot(_v^) != 0, \] and therefore $_v^$ cannot bound a Lagrangian disk inside $B_(v)$. In particular, the vertex is not locally Lagrangian smoothable.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: Connects polyhedral Maslov index to Legendrian rotation/Thurston–Bennequin invariants and local Lagrangian smoothability; central to symplectic/contact topology with concrete structural consequences for smoothing problems.
viability_rationale: Conjecture stems from LLM heuristics and authors list several delicate technical gaps (Maslov identification, tb computation); resolving requires careful analytic/topological work, making short-term success uncertain.
rationale: The statement is presented as a conjecture (attributed to ChatGPT) and the authors state they could not verify the calculation, listing gaps to be filled for a proof.
evidence_snippet: Conjecture appears as "Conj [ChatGPT 5.2]" with the authors noting "Due to our time budget we could not work to verify this calculation." (lines 548–578, 580–586).

title: New irreducible components of $\mathcal{B}(0,c_2)$ and Computation of the Dimension of its tangent space
plain_text: Let \(a\) and \(k\) be positive integers. For each integer \(b in 0,1,,k-1 \) with \(a>b\), there exists a stable rank-2 vector bundle \(E\) arising as the cohomology of a minimal monad of the form equation (a, b,k) : 0 O_ P^ 3 (-a) ^ k matrix O_ P^ 3 (a-1) ^ k O_ P^ 3 (b) O_ P^ 3 (1-a) ^ k _ P^ 3 (-b) matrix O_ P^ 3 (a) ^ k 0. equation Moreover, for all sufficiently large integers \(a 0\), the corresponding family of such bundles belongs to an irreducible component of \(B(0,k(2a-1)-b^2)\), whose dimension exceeds the expected dimension.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: Predicts infinite families of stable rank‑2 bundles from monads giving new, unexpectedly large components of the moduli; of solid interest in moduli theory but largely a technical generalization of constructed examples.
viability_rationale: Constructive monad families proven for k=1,2 with computational evidence and partial k=3 results. Extending constructions and deformation counts seems attainable in the near term.
rationale: The paper formulates this existence statement for families of stable bundles and large a as a conjecture; only special cases (k=1, k=2 and partial k=3) are established, not the full claim.

Moreover, for all sufficiently large a, the corresponding family belongs to an irreducible component ... whose dimension exceeds the expected dimension.' Partial cases k=1,2 proven.

title: Nonlinear methods for tensors: determinantal equations for secant varieties beyond cactus
body_tex:
\label{conj:tangency}
    Let $\CC[x_1,\dots,x_d]\surjto R$ be a surjection to an $n$-dimensional algebra $R$, let $[R]$ be the corresponding point of $\Hilb_n\CC^d$ and let $T$ be the structure tensor of $R$. Then
    \[
        \rank\tang^C(T) - n(n-1)(n-2) = dn - \dim\T_{[R]}\Hilb_n\CC^d.
    \]
plain_text: Let $ R$ be a surjection to an $n$-dimensional algebra $R$, let $[R]$ be the corresponding point of $_n^d$ and let $T$ be the structure tensor of $R$. Then \[ ^C(T) - n(n-1)(n-2) = dn - _ [R] _n^d. \]
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: Links determinantal ranks of tangency flattenings to Hilbert-scheme tangent dimensions, giving a structural bridge between tensor equations and scheme geometry with broad methodological impact.
viability_rationale: General relation linking tangency-flattening rank to Hilbert-scheme tangent dimensions is natural and supported by examples, but proving in full generality seems technically challenging.
rationale: Conjecture is introduced in the paper's Future Directions and is not proved here; it remains an open prediction about tangency flattening rank equals a Hilbert-scheme tangent-space defect.
evidence_snippet: Conjecture (Conj. 1): For structure tensor T of algebra R, rk tang^C(T)-n(n-1)(n-2)=dn - dim T_[R] Hilb_n C^d. Stated as a conjecture in Future Directions.

title: Nonlinear methods for tensors: determinantal equations for secant varieties beyond cactus

Let $\CC[x_1,\dots,x_d]\surjto R$ be a surjection to an $n$-dimensional Gorenstein algebra $R$, let $[R]$ be the corresponding point of $\Hilb_n\CC^d$ and let $T$ be the structure tensor of $R$. Then
    \[
        \rank\Phi_{\mathrm M,3}^C(T) - \binom{n}{3} = dn - \dim\T_{[R]}\Hilb_n\CC^d.
    \]

label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: Predicts exact rank deficit of a specific minor flattening for Gorenstein algebra structure tensors, tying tensor determinantal invariants to tangent dimensions on the Hilbert scheme.
viability_rationale: A more specific Gorenstein-version has supporting computations and symmetry, yet remains a deep Hilbert-scheme/rank identity; plausible partial progress but full resolution uncertain in five years.
rationale: Paper explicitly states this Gorenstein version as a conjecture in the Future Directions/Hilbert schemes section and does not prove it.
evidence_snippet: Conjecture: For Gorenstein algebra R with structure tensor T, rk Phi_{M,3}^C(T)-binom(n,3)=dn - dim T_[R] Hilb_n C^d. Presented as an open conjecture.

title: Graph bootstrap percolation -- a discovery of slowness
body_tex:
\label{conj:K5}
 [Bollob\'as-Przykucki-Riordan-Sahasrabudhe~\cite{bollobas2017maximum}] The maximum running time for infection rule $K_5$ is $M_{K_5}(n)=o(n^2)$.
plain_text: [Bollob\'as-Przykucki-Riordan-Sahasrabudhe ] The maximum running time for infection rule $K_5$ is $M_ K_5 (n)=o(n^2)$.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: A central open problem in bootstrap percolation: the K5 maximum running time asymptotics; resolving it would decisively inform extremal dynamics and links to additive combinatorics.
viability_rationale: Active area with near-quadratic lower bounds (n^{2-o(1)}); narrowing to o(n^2) is a delicate extremal-combinatorics problem with substantial remaining obstacles.
rationale: Survey cites the Bollobás–Przykucki–Riordan–Sahasrabudhe conjecture and notes it remains the main outstanding question; the paper and later constructions give near-quadratic lower bounds but do not resolve o(n^2).
evidence_snippet: Conjecture (from Bollobás et al.): The maximum running time for infection rule K_5 is M_{K_5}(n)=o(n^2). Stated as the central outstanding question; not resolved here.

title: On the Calderón sum formula for wavelet systems

label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: Central open problem in wavelet/frame theory (Bownik–Lemvig conjecture); proving Calderón formula for arbitrary dilations/translations would clarify fundamental characterizations of Parseval/orthonormal wavelet systems.
viability_rationale: Paper gives a new approach proving the Calderón formula for arbitrary A,P under a mild ψ condition, partially resolving the conjecture; plausible further technical work could remove the assumption within five years, but gaps remain.
rationale: The conjecture is cited from prior work and the paper proves the Calderón formula only under an extra mild assumption (ψ ∈ B_π), so the exact unconstrained statement remains unresolved.
evidence_snippet: Lines 152-156,165-171: 'This partially solves a conjecture by Bownik and Lemvig.' and Conjecture statement asserting the Calderón sum formula.

title: Integrals and Rigidity on Manifolds with Nonnegative Ricci Curvature
plain_text: For a complete non-compact Riemannian manifold $M^3$ with $Rc >= 0$ and maximum volume growth, there is align _ r _ B_q(r) R r =8 . align

interestingness_rationale: An asymptotic scalar-curvature integral formula linking maximal-volume growth to geometric cone data in 3D Ricci-nonnegative manifolds; significant for rigidity and geometric analysis within the field.
viability_rationale: Partial results exist (pole case, related rigidity theorems) and techniques (GH convergence, Ricci flow) are active; main obstacle is justifying key convergence, a technical but approachable analytic problem.
rationale: The integral formula is posed as a conjecture; authors note it was proved for manifolds with a pole elsewhere but do not prove the general case here.
evidence_snippet: Lines 193-197: conjectured limit equals 8π(1−V_M). Lines 201-204: remark it's proven for manifolds with pole in [Xu]; general case remains conjectural.

title: Integrals and Rigidity on Manifolds with Nonnegative Ricci Curvature
plain_text: If $(M^3, g)$ is a complete, non-compact Riemannian manifold and Ricci pinched, then $(M^3, g)$ is flat.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: Hamilton's 3D Ricci-pinching conjecture is a central problem in Ricci-flow and 3‑manifold geometry; many partial proofs exist but full generality remains open—resolution would yield substantial structural consequences.
viability_rationale: Active progress: multiple recent Ricci-flow and inverse-mean-curvature arguments verify special cases; this paper proves a maximal-volume-growth case. Remaining general obstructions are technical but approachable, making resolution plausible within five years.
rationale: Paper states Hamilton's pinching conjecture as open; notes partial verifications under extra assumptions and the authors prove only a special case (maximal volume growth), so the full conjecture remains unresolved.
evidence_snippet: Lines 206-213: 'Hamilton has proposed the following conjecture... The conjecture has been verified with additional assumption by [CZ] and [Lott]. ... As an application ... we provide an alternative proof of a special case of Hamilton's pinching conjecture in dimension three.'

title: New Types of Sturm bounds via $p$-adic transfer methods
body_tex: Let $k \geq 1$ be an integer, and let $f \in \widetilde{M}_{2k}$. Then $f$ is determined uniquely by its first $\dim \widetilde{M}_{2k}$ Fourier coefficients. Furthermore, $f \pmod{m}$ for any $m \geq 2$ is uniquely determined by its first $\dim \widetilde{M}_{2k}$ coefficients modulo $m$.
plain_text: Let $k >= 1$ be an integer, and let $f in M_ 2k $. Then $f$ is determined uniquely by its first $ M_ 2k $ Fourier coefficients. Furthermore, $f m$ for any $m >= 2$ is uniquely determined by its first $ M_ 2k $ coefficients modulo $m$.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: An optimal Sturm-type bound for quasimodular forms extending classical Sturm's theorem; valuable for computations and congruences but specialized to modular/quasimodular arithmetic.
viability_rationale: Strong computational evidence up to k≤20, existing depth-1 results, and clear proposed routes (valence formula/triangular bases) make a proof plausible within five years.
rationale: Author explicitly labels this statement as a conjecture about optimal Sturm bounds for quasimodular forms and leaves it unproven in the paper.
evidence_snippet: Lines 963–965 present the conjecture and the following discussion (line 967) indicates it is left as an open question.

title: Remarks on modules of finite projective dimension

viability_rationale: Paper proves several special cases (pd≤1, depth hypotheses) but full replacement of regularity by pd(M)<∞ is nontrivial; plausible progress but still a substantive gap.
rationale: The paper explicitly states this as a conjecture and does not prove it; no counterexample or proof is given here.

interestingness_rationale: Meaningful Auslander-type strengthening: reflexivity of M⊗M implying reflexivity of M under finite projective dimension; useful but focused within module/reflexivity theory with partial corollaries provided.
viability_rationale: Authors obtain meaningful special-case results (depth 3, additional Hom reflexive hypotheses); general conjecture remains delicate though approachable with further partial advances.
rationale: Authors pose the exact statement as a conjecture (lines 441–443); subsequent results give partial cases but do not prove the general statement.
evidence_snippet: Lines 441–443: 'If \pd(M)<\infty and M\otimes_R M is reflexive, then M is reflexive.' Followed by corollaries and partial results, not a proof.

title: Primes represented by quadratic forms and the Weil abscissa of abelian profinite groups
body_tex: The shifted partial Riemann zeta function $\zeta_S(s-1):= \prod_{p \in S} \frac{1}{1-p^{-s+1}}$ has the same abscissa of convergence as $\zeta^W_{H_S}(s)$.
plain_text: The shifted partial Riemann zeta function $_S(s-1):= _ p in S 1 1-p^ -s+1 $ has the same abscissa of convergence as $^W_ H_S (s)$.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: Relates the Weil abscissa of a procyclic abelian group to a shifted partial Riemann zeta function; a meaningful specialist conjecture tying representation-growth invariants to prime distribution in progressions.
viability_rationale: Concrete analytic-number-theory question tied to prime distribution; authors proved key cases. Expect progress for more specific S, but full general conjecture depends on delicate prime-distribution inputs.
rationale: The authors prove the equality for three specific arithmetic-progression sets S, but the general conjecture (equality for arbitrary S) is not proved and remains open.
evidence_snippet: Lines 125-128: Conjecture A predicts equality of abscissae. Lines 117-123 and 139-143: theorem confirms three cases; authors note further cases remain open.

title: On the Hilbert depth of the quotient ring of the edge ideal of a complete bipartite graph
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: Elementary numerical product inequalities used to derive a sharper Hilbert depth bound; narrow, computation-motivated and mainly of technical interest to this paper's estimates.
viability_rationale: Elementary analytic product inequalities with numerical evidence; reducible to harmonic/digamma bounds and exponential estimates. Likely provable by standard calculus/inequality techniques within a few years.
rationale: This product-inequality conjecture is proposed here based on computer experiments and is assumed (not proved) in later propositions, so it remains an open conjecture in this paper.
evidence_snippet: Lines 557–572: The authors propose Conjecture (product inequalities) from experiments and use it as an assumption in Proposition 5.1, with no proof provided.

title: Temperley-Lieb Immanants, Key Positivity, and Demazure Crystals
body_tex: Every Demazure crystal $\mathcal{B}_{w, \lambda}$ (in the type $A$, normal, finite case) is isomorphic to a crystal of flagged skew tableaux for some shape $\lambda/\mu$ and nondecreasing flag $\vec{b}$. This would imply that all results in this appendix hold for every Demazure crystal.
plain_text: Every Demazure crystal $B_ w, $ (in the type $A$, normal, finite case) is isomorphic to a crystal of flagged skew tableaux for some shape $/$ and nondecreasing flag $b$. This would imply that all results in this appendix hold for every Demazure crystal.

title: Tree Pairs for Algebraic Bieri-Strebel Groups
body_tex: A well defined tree-pair for an algebraic Bieri-Strebel group \FN{\beta} can only be derived from an associated subdivision polynomial $P(x)$ if $P(x)$ is the minimal polynomial with the root $\beta$ among polynomials of the form $a_nx^n + ... + a_1x -1$, $a_i \in \mathbb{Z}_{\geq 0}$.
plain_text: A well defined tree-pair for an algebraic Bieri-Strebel group can only be derived from an associated subdivision polynomial $P(x)$ if $P(x)$ is the minimal polynomial with the root $$ among polynomials of the form $a_nx^n + ... + a_1x -1$, $a_i in Z_ >= 0 $.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: Connects existence of tree-pair representations to minimality of the subdivision polynomial; a nontrivial structural conjecture for algebraic Bieri–Strebel groups with meaningful but narrowly scoped consequences.
viability_rationale: Specific algebraic/group-theoretic conjecture with evidence for low degrees but requires new structural arguments; plausible progress but nontrivial general proof uncertain in a 5-year window.

title: Dynamical Mordell-Lang conjecture for split self-maps of affine curve times projective curve
body_tex: Let $f:X\to X$ be an endomorphism of a quasi-projective variety over a field $K$ of characteristic 0, and $V$ be a closed subvariety of $X$. Then for every $x\in X(K)$, the return set $\{ n\in \mathbb{N}|\  f^n(x) \in V(K)\}$ is a finite union of arithmetic progressions.
plain_text: Let $f:X to X$ be an endomorphism of a quasi-projective variety over a field $K$ of characteristic 0, and $V$ be a closed subvariety of $X$. Then for every $x in X(K)$, the return set $ n in N|\ f^n(x) in V(K) $ is a finite union of arithmetic progressions.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: The Dynamical Mordell–Lang conjecture is a central, longstanding problem in arithmetic dynamics about orbit intersections; resolving it has deep consequences across Diophantine geometry and dynamical systems.
viability_rationale: Dynamical Mordell–Lang is a longstanding, widely open conjecture. Many special cases (étale, A^2, and this paper's affine×projective-curve case) are solved, but a full general resolution in 5 years is unlikely.
rationale: The quoted statement is the full Dynamical Mordell–Lang conjecture for arbitrary quasi-projective varieties; the paper proves only a special case (affine curve × projective curve), not the general conjecture.
evidence_snippet: Lines 122-124 state the general DML conjecture; Theorem (line 137-139) proves DML for X affine curve and Y projective curve over Qbar.

title: Ekedahl-Oort Types and Newton Polygons of Abelian Covers of $\mathbf{P}^1$ Branched at Three Points
body_tex:
\label{unlikely-density}
    Let $g \geq 18$, and let $m$ be the least common multiple of the exponents of all of the Galois groups of abelian covers of $\mathbf{P}^1$ branched at three points of genus $g$. If $p$ is a prime such that $p \not \equiv 1$ modulo $m$, then there exist abelian covers $X_1$ and $X_2$ of $\mathbf{P}^1$ branched at three points defined over $\overline{\mathbf{F}_p}$ such that $X_1$ has an unlikely Newton polygon and $X_2$ has an unlikely Ekedahl-Oort type.
plain_text: Let $g >= 18$, and let $m$ be the least common multiple of the exponents of all of the Galois groups of abelian covers of $P^1$ branched at three points of genus $g$. If $p$ is a prime such that $p 1$ modulo $m$, then there exist abelian covers $X_1$ and $X_2$ of $P^1$ branched at three points defined over $ F_p $ such that $X_1$ has an unlikely Newton polygon and $X_2$ has an unlikely Ekedahl-Oort type.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: A density statement about primes giving abelian three-point covers with unlikely Newton/E-O invariants: meaningful within arithmetic geometry but family-specific and supported largely by computational evidence.
viability_rationale: Concrete computational evidence and specific family raise prospects, but the uniform existence statement across primes is strong and hinges on number-theoretic/group-theoretic obstacles.
rationale: Authors present this density conjecture based on computations and do not prove it; it is posed as an open conjecture in the paper.
evidence_snippet: Lines 86-90: "The data we collected led us to make the following conjecture..." followed by the statement; no proof given.

title: Flips for spaces of quadrics on del Pezzo varieties

Then there exists a semiorthogonal decomposition equation ((2,)) = (S,_0), , (S,_0)_ n+1 times , (S), , (S)_ (n-1)(n+1) 2 times , equation where $(S,_0)$ is the derived category of the sheaf of even Clifford algebras over the base $S$ associated to the quadric fibration $ to S$.

title: Almost all graphs are vertex-minor universal
body_tex: There exists $C\in [\sqrt{2\log_2(4/3)},\sqrt{2\log_2(3)}]$ such that for any $\eps > 0$, with high probability $G\sim \bG(n,1/2)$ is $(C-\eps)\sqrt n$-vertex-minor universal and not $(C+\eps)\sqrt n$-vertex-minor universal.

label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: Predicts a sharp universality threshold (explicit constant) in G(n,1/2); proving it would be an important probabilistic-combinatorics result about vertex-minor universality and threshold behavior.
viability_rationale: Authors prove matching Θ(√n) bounds (constants ~0.911 and ~1.78). Closing this gap to a single sharp constant likely needs new techniques; moderate chance of resolution within five years given partial results.

title: Almost all graphs are vertex-minor universal
body_tex:
\label{conj:p neq 1/2}
    If $p = \omega(1/\sqrt{n})$ is at most $1/2$ and $G \sim \bG(n,p)$ or $G \sim \bG(n,1-p)$, then $G$ is $k$-vertex-minor universal with high probability for some $k = \Omega(p\sqrt n)$.

title: Quantitative partitioned index theorem and noncompact band-width
body_tex:
\label{con band width cpt}
Let $N$ be a compact manifold without boundary, of dimension $n-1 \geq 5$. Suppose that $N$ does not admit Riemannian metrics of positive scalar curvature. Consider a Riemannian metric $g$ on $N \times [-1,1]$. Let $\ell$ be the distance between $N \times \{-1\}$ and $N \times \{1\}$ with respect to this metric. Then
\[
	\inf_{x\in N \times [-1,1]}\Sc_g(x)\le\frac{4\pi^2(n-1)}{n\ell^2}.
\]
plain_text: Let $N$ be a compact manifold without boundary, of dimension $n-1 >= 5$. Suppose that $N$ does not admit Riemannian metrics of positive scalar curvature. Consider a Riemannian metric $g$ on $N x [-1,1]$. Let $ ell $ be the distance between $N x -1 $ and $N x 1 $ with respect to this metric. Then \[ _ x in N x [-1,1] _g(x) <= 4^2(n-1) n ell ^2 . \]
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: A central conjecture in scalar-curvature geometry (Gromov's band-width). Proving it in full beyond Rosenberg-index cases would have significant structural and rigidity consequences for positive scalar curvature metrics.
viability_rationale: Active partial results (Cecchini, Zeidler) cover Rosenberg-index cases; authors extend techniques to noncompact settings, but full Gromov band-width conjecture remains a broad geometric-analytic challenge, unlikely fully settled in 5 years.
rationale: Gromov's band-width conjecture is stated and remains open in full generality; only partial results (e.g. under nonzero Rosenberg index) have been proved by Cecchini and Zeidler.
evidence_snippet: Lines 229–236 and 239–252: "Gromov made the following conjecture... Versions of Gromov's conjecture were proved by Zeidler and Cecchini (under Rosenberg index nonzero)."

title: On a conjecture by Michael Wemyss regarding the calculation of GV invariants
plain_text: Let $A$ be a contraction algebra and $d = _ A$. Then a point $[A]$ corresponding to $A$ in the moduli space $_d$ of associative unital algebras (see ) is smooth, in other words, $A$ is unobstructed, the dimension of the centre is constant in an open neighbourhood of $[A]$ and the associated map $A maps to Z(A)$ is smooth.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: Unobstructedness of contraction algebras would structurally connect noncommutative deformations to commutative theory and enable intrinsic GV invariant calculations, with substantial impact in 3-fold flop and deformation theory.
viability_rationale: Authors proved types A and D; extending to all contraction algebras requires deeper deformation-theoretic control and new tools, plausible but nontrivial.
rationale: The conjecture about unobstructedness and constancy/smoothness of the centre is stated as a new open conjecture; the paper proves cases (types A and D) and small d checks but not the general statement.
evidence_snippet: Lines 149-158, 175-179: 'We formulate this precisely in the following conjecture... Let A be a contraction algebra... [A] is smooth... the dimension of the centre is constant... We verified ... for small values of d ... the conjecture does not follow from vanishing of HH^3(A,A).'

title: The Ising magnetisation field and the Gaussian free field

\label{conj:AT-mag-field}
	 The statement of Theorem~\ref{thm:main-decomp} under the almost verbatim analogue of the iterations \hyperlink{pos}{$(+)$} and \hyperlink{free}{$(\text{free})$} holds for the Ashkin--Teller magnetisation field defined by~\eqref{eq:fielddef} at each point of the critical line~\eqref{eq:critical}, the only difference being that every explicit occurrence of $\sqrt 2$ has to be again replaced with $\sqrt g$ defined in~\eqref{eq:defg}.
plain_text: The statement of Theorem under the almost verbatim analogue of the iterations pos $(+)$ and free $(free)$ holds for the Ashkin--Teller magnetisation field defined by at each point of the critical line , the only difference being that every explicit occurrence of $ 2$ has to be again replaced with $ g$ defined in .
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: Extends the continuum geometric decomposition of magnetisation fields to the Ashkin–Teller line; important for continuum AT field theory but contingent on other convergence conjectures.
viability_rationale: Follows from Conj. 631 and height-function convergence; authors and others (Aru–Lupu) have partial results, making this moderately likely if prior conjectures are resolved.
rationale: Conjecture~\ref{conj:AT-mag-field} is explicitly posed and the paper only proves partial analytic facts (Proposition~\ref{prop:AT-mag-field}), not the full conjectured decomposition.

title: The Ising magnetisation field and the Gaussian free field
body_tex:
\label{conj:AT-inter}
	Consider the clusters of the decomposition described in Conjecture \ref{conj:AT-IGMC} under $+/+$ boundary conditions. Toss a fair coin for each cluster to assign a value $+1$ or $-1$, except for the boundary cluster which is assigned $+1$. If two clusters with the value $+1$ (resp. $-1$) touch each other, we say that they belong to the same $\oplus$-cluster (resp. $\ominus$-cluster). Then, the law of the outermost interfaces of the Ashkin-Teller magnetisation field is given by the outermost outer boundaries of the $\ominus$-clusters. The full collection of interfaces is defined iteratively.
plain_text: Consider the clusters of the decomposition described in Conjecture under $+/+$ boundary conditions. Toss a fair coin for each cluster to assign a value $+1$ or $-1$, except for the boundary cluster which is assigned $+1$. If two clusters with the value $+1$ (resp. $-1$) touch each other, we say that they belong to the same $$-cluster (resp. $$-cluster). Then, the law of the outermost interfaces of the Ashkin-Teller magnetisation field is given by the outermost outer boundaries of the $$-clusters. The full collection of interfaces is defined iteratively.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: Describes a natural continuum percolation construction of AT interfaces via coin-flipped cluster signs; interesting structural picture but narrower and conditional on earlier conjectures.
viability_rationale: More geometric and iterative, relying on earlier conjectures; plausible conditional on those but more speculative and technically delicate, lowering near-term solvability.
rationale: The continuum percolation description of AT interfaces is stated as Conjecture~\ref{conj:AT-inter} and is not proved in the paper.
evidence_snippet: Lines 670–672: 'Then, the law of the outermost interfaces of the Ashkin-Teller magnetisation field is given by the outermost outer boundaries of the \ominus-clusters.' (Conjecture~\ref{conj:AT-inter}.)

title: Transverse expansion of the metric at null infinity
body_tex:
\label{conjecture}
Let $\{\scri,\bg,\bm\ell,\elltwo,\sigma,\mf q\}$ be $\scri$-structure data of odd dimension $\mf n\ge 7$ admitting a cross-section $\iota:\Sigma \hookrightarrow\scri$ with induced metric $h\d\iota^{\star}\bg$ and let $\mc{O}_{ab}^{\scri}$ be the radiative obstruction tensor. Denote by $\mc{O}^{FG}$ the Fefferman-Graham obstruction tensor of $[h]$. Then, $$\iota^{\star}\big(\text{\pounds}_n^{(\frac{\mf n-3}{2})} \mc{O}^{\scri} \big) = c_{\mf n} \, \mc{O}^{FG},$$ where $c_{\mf n}$ is a constant depending only on $\mf n$.

Denote by $O^ FG $ the Fefferman-Graham obstruction tensor of $[h]$. Then, $$^ (_n^ ( n-3 2 ) O^ ) = c_ n \, O^ FG ,$$ where $c_ n $ is a constant depending only on $ n$.

interestingness_rationale: Connects the radiative obstruction at null infinity to the Fefferman–Graham obstruction tensor in higher odd dimensions; supported by low-d evidence and would clarify asymptotic geometric structure in relativity and conformal geometry.
viability_rationale: Strong evidence from lower dimensions and ambient-metric work; conjecture reduces to detailed control of lower-order terms — technical but plausibly resolvable by specialists within a few years.
