rationale: The authors pose the induced-minor balanced-separator statement as an explicit conjecture; the paper proves a related fat-minor result but not this induced-minor conjecture.
evidence_snippet: Conjecture: For every graph H there exist constants c,r such that every n-vertex weighted graph G that excludes H as an induced minor has a balanced separator that is (c\sqrt{n},r)-coverable.

title: Matching and intersection problems for non-trivial $r$-partite $r$-uniform hypergraphs
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: An exact extremal formula for specific parameter ranges in non-trivial r-partite r-graphs: valuable within extremal combinatorics but a narrow, parameter-specific completion.
viability_rationale: Concrete extremal combinatorics formula with many resolved cases in the paper; remaining cases look approachable with current techniques, giving moderate near-term viability.
rationale: This formula is stated as a conjecture of Lu and Ma; the paper confirms some cases (large n and specific t) but does not prove the full conjecture for all parameters.
evidence_snippet: Conjecture: For r-2\ge t\ge 1, if n\ge 2, then \iota_0(t,n;r)=\max\{n^{r-t}-(n-1)^{r-t}+t(n-1),(t+2)n^{r-t-1}-(t+1)n^{r-t-2}\}.

title: Cusp Form Dimensions, Lattice Uniqueness, and LP Sharpness for Sphere Packing in Dimensions 8 and 24
body_tex:
\label{conj:primal}
If the Cohn--Elkies LP bound is sharp in dimension~$d$ (with $d \equiv 0 \pmod{8}$), then $\dim S_{d/2}(\SL_2(\Z)) \leq 1$.
plain_text: If the Cohn--Elkies LP bound is sharp in dimension $d$ (with $d 0 8$), then $ S_ d/2 (_2()) <= 1$.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: Connects LP sharpness for sphere packing (d≡0 mod 8) to the dimension of cusp form spaces, forging a deep bridge between modular forms and packing optimality with strong classification consequences.
viability_rationale: Conjectured implication ties LP sharpness to cusp-form dimension with supporting heuristics; modular/CFT tools might yield progress but proving the general implication remains a deep challenge.
rationale: The author formulates this implication as a conjecture linking LP sharpness and modular form dimension; the paper does not prove the equivalence, so it remains open.
evidence_snippet: Paper: 'We formulate a conjecture that these three conditions are equivalent...' (no proof of the stated implication is given).

title: On the Sum of Squares of Irreducible Brauer Character Degrees in Blocks
body_tex:
\label{MRconj}
    Let \(G\) be a finite group and \(b\) a block of \(G\), then \(l(b) \leq p^{s(b)}\).
plain_text: Let \(G\) be a finite group and \(b\) a block of \(G\), then \(l(b) <= p^ s(b) \).
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: Natural structural inequality for block invariants; reduction to quasi-simple groups under abelian defect indicates meaningful content in modular representation theory.
viability_rationale: Nontrivial reduction to quasi-simple groups and p=2 resolved under abelian defect gives concrete progress, but remaining odd primes and non-abelian defect cases remain substantial.
rationale: Paper studies a weakened Holm–Willems conjecture and reduces cases; proves p=2 and some reductions, but does not prove the general inequality for all blocks.
evidence_snippet: Abstract: 'We study a weakened version of the Holm--Willems Local Conjecture. The problem is reduced to quasi-simple groups under the assumption that the defect group is abelian. Complete proofs are provided in the case p = 2.'

title: Deformations, Derived Categories, and Multiparameter Persistence: A Theoretical Framework
plain_text: There exist constants $C_1, C_2 > 0$, independent of $M$ and $N$, such that for all persistence modules $M, N$ over a poset $P$, $$ C_1 d_ DG (M, N) <= d_I(M, N) <= C_2 d_ DG (M, N). $$ In other words, the interleaving distance $d_I$ and the derived metric $d_ DG $ are bilipschitz equivalent.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: A bilipschitz link between interleaving and derived metrics would unify stability theory and derived-category methods, a substantial conceptual advance for multiparameter persistence.
viability_rationale: Statement over all posets is strong but likely decidable soon via counterexamples or restricted-class proofs; active, recent tools may yield resolution.
rationale: The conjecture (bilipschitz equivalence between interleaving and derived metrics) is proposed by the authors and not proved in the paper; they formulate it as an open problem.
evidence_snippet: Conjecture (Derived Interleaving Metric): 'There exist constants C1, C2>0 ... C1 d_DG(M,N) <= d_I(M,N) <= C2 d_DG(M,N).'

title: Fundamental fields in the deformed $W$-algebras
body_tex:
\label{conj1}
For each $i = 1, \ldots, \ell$, there exists a field $T_i(z)$ in $\mathbf{W}_{q,t}(\mathfrak{g})$, such that $T_i(z) = Y_i(z) +$ the sum of elementary terms of the form
\[
    c(q,t) : Y_i(z) A_{i_1}(zq^{a_1}t^{b_1})^{-1} \cdots A_{i_k}(zq^{a_k}t^{b_k})^{-1} :
\]
(where $c(q,1)$ is a positive integer independent of $q$). Furthermore, the set of weights of these terms counted with multiplicity $c(q,1)$ is the set of weights of the finite-dimensional irreducible representation $V_{\omega_i}$ of $U_q(\widehat{\mathfrak{g}})$ with highest weight $\omega_i$, where the weight of $$ :\prod_{j=1}^dY_{i_j}(za_j)^{\varepsilon_j}:$$
is $$\sum_{j=1}^d \varepsilon_j \omega_{i_j}.$$
plain_text: For each $i = 1, , ell $, there exists a field $T_i(z)$ in $W_ q,t (g)$, such that $T_i(z) = Y_i(z) +$ the sum of elementary terms of the form \[ c(q,t) : Y_i(z) A_ i_1 (zq^ a_1 t^ b_1 )^ -1 A_ i_k (zq^ a_k t^ b_k )^ -1 : \] (where $c(q,1)$ is a positive integer independent of $q$). Furthermore, the set of weights of these terms counted with multiplicity $c(q,1)$ is the set of weights of the finite-dimensional irreducible representation $V_ _i $ of $U_q( g )$ with highest weight $_i$, where the weight of $$ :_ j=1 ^dY_ i_j (za_j)^ _j :$$ is $$_ j=1 ^d _j _ i_j .$$
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: Connects deformed W-algebras to weights of finite-dimensional U_q(âg) modules; a structural bridge in quantum affine/W-algebra representation theory, proven in some types but open generally.
viability_rationale: Partial proofs in types B and C and an explicit algorithmic framework reduce the gap; full uniform proof across all types remains nontrivial, so moderate near-term solvability is plausible but uncertain.
rationale: Paper proves Frenkel–Reshetikhin conjecture only in types B_ell, C_ell and some nodes; the full general statement for all simple g remains unresolved.
evidence_snippet: Abstract: 'we... apply this algorithm to... prove a conjecture of Frenkel and Reshetikhin... in types B_ell, C_ell, and for some nodes in other types.'

title: Colour-balanced subgraphs
body_tex:
Let $\vecf \colon E(K_{n,n}) \to \mathbb{R}^k$ such that $\| \vecf(e) \|_1 \leq 1$ for all $e \in E(K_{n,n})$. Then there is a perfect matching $M$ of $K_{n,n}$ satisfying
    \[
        f_\vecf(M) = \bigO(\sqrt{k}).
    \]
plain_text: Let $ E(K_ n,n ) to R^k$ such that $\| (e) \|_1 <= 1$ for all $e in E(K_ n,n )$. Then there is a perfect matching $M$ of $K_ n,n $ satisfying \[ f_(M) = (k). \]
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: A natural discrepancy-type improvement (O(sqrt{k})) for perfect matchings connects to necklace-splitting and discrepancy theory; broadly relevant across combinatorics and theoretical CS.
viability_rationale: Discrepancy-style conjecture with partial progress here (linear relaxation, necklace-splitting). Closing the gap to O(√k) likely needs new Banaszczyk/rounding ideas; plausible but nontrivial within five years.
rationale: The authors pose the sqrt(k) bound for perfect matchings as a conjecture; the paper does not prove this optimal bound (it improves but does not settle it).
evidence_snippet: Conjecture: Let f: E(K_{n,n}) → R^k with ||f(e)||_1 ≤ 1 for all e. Then there is a perfect matching M of K_{n,n} satisfying f_f(M) = O(√k).

title: Toric schemes and integral models for Shimura varieties with $Γ_1(p)$-type level
body_tex:
\label{conj:torsors}
 There exists a $T_\CG$-torsor $P$ over $\CS_{K_0}$ together with an isomorphism of $T_\CG$-torsors over 
 $\CS_{K_0}[1/p]={\rm Sh}_{\RK_0}(\RG, \RX)_E$,
 \[
 \alpha: P[1/p]\xrightarrow{\sim} Q_\pi,
 \]
  and an isomorphism of pairs 
 \[
 \beta: \big(L_*(P), L_*(\alpha^{-1})\circ a_\pi: T_{\CG}[1/p]\xrightarrow{\sim} L_*(P)[1/p]\big)\xrightarrow{\sim} \varphi^*\big(\RP^{(-1)}_{\CG,\mu}, s_{\CG,\mu}\big),
 \]
 where the pair  $(\RP_{\CG,\mu}, s_{\CG,\mu})$ is as in Conjecture \ref{divconj} and the pull-back is by the local model diagram morphism $\varphi: \CS_{K_0}\to \fkM_{\CG,\mu}
 =[\CG\bs \Mloc_{\CG,\mu}]$.
plain_text: There exists a $T_$-torsor $P$ over $_ K_0 $ together with an isomorphism of $T_$-torsors over $_ K_0 [1/p]= Sh _ _0 (, )_E$, \[ : P[1/p] sim Q_, \] and an isomorphism of pairs \[ : (L_*(P), L_*(^ -1 ) a_: T_ [1/p] sim L_*(P)[1/p]) sim ^*(^ (-1) _ , , s_ , ), \] where the pair $(_ , , s_ , )$ is as in Conjecture and the pull-back is by the local model diagram morphism $: _ K_0 to _ , =[ _ , ]$.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: Posits a canonical T_G-torsor over the integral model matching the unramified cover and local-model data; a technical but crucial compatibility statement needed to construct the desired integral models.
viability_rationale: Authors prove the conjecture for PEL type with Iwahori; it hinges on the local-model/divisor theorem and toric constructions. Methods seem extendable to many cases, but full generality likely needs additional new input.
rationale: Conjecture \ref{conj:torsors} is presented as an open conjecture (existence of the T_\CG-torsor P with specified isomorphisms); the paper does not claim a general proof here.
evidence_snippet: Conjecture (lines 3754-3766): 'There exists a T_\CG-torsor P over \CS_{K_0} ...' followed by discussion and uniqueness remark, but no general proof is given in these lines.

title: Landau Analysis in the Grassmannian
body_tex:
\label{conj:rat_formula}
Given any rational Landau diagram $\mathcal{L}$, the LS discriminant on each irreducible component of $V_G$ can be expressed as in~\eqref{eq:discr_tr_fact}, and similarly the mixed LS discriminant as in~\eqref{eq:discr_tr_fact_mixed}. Both products run over all external colorings that are admissible for $\mathcal{L}$.
plain_text: Given any rational Landau diagram $L$, the LS discriminant on each irreducible component of $V_G$ can be expressed as in , and similarly the mixed LS discriminant as in . Both products run over all external colorings that are admissible for $L$.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: Predicts a uniform factorization pattern for LS discriminants across rational Landau diagrams, tying algebraic geometry to Feynman singularity structure; meaningful within amplitudes and discriminant theory.
viability_rationale: Authors prove triangle case and present structural degree counts; generalizing via similar algebraic/degeneration methods seems feasible, so moderate chance of a near-term resolution.
rationale: Authors state the factorization formula as a conjecture for general rational Landau diagrams, give examples and evidence, but do not prove the general statement.
evidence_snippet: Lines 2194-2199: "We expect Theorem... to generalize... [Conjecture: Given any rational Landau diagram, the LS discriminant on each irreducible component can be expressed as in ...]."

title: Acyclic sets and colorings in digraphs under restrictions on degrees and cycle lengths
body_tex:
If $D$ is an oriented graph with no directed cycle of length greater than $s$, 
then $\vec{\chi}(D) = O(s / \log s)$ as $s\rightarrow\infty$.
plain_text: If $D$ is an oriented graph with no directed cycle of length greater than $s$, then $(D) = O(s / s)$ as $s$.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: Suggests an improved dichromatic-number bound (O(s/log s)) for orientations of bounded circumference; meaningful within digraph coloring but narrower than major field-level conjectures.
viability_rationale: Concrete extremal combinatorics conjecture with only linear bounds known (≤s/2). Plausible probabilistic/structural attack but no explicit reductions or partial breakthroughs given—moderate but uncertain chance of resolution within 5 years.
rationale: Authors propose this circumference versus dichromatic bounds conjecture after giving weaker bounds; no proof or counterexample is provided in the paper, so it remains open.
evidence_snippet: Lines 366–369: corollary gives vecχ(D) ≤ ⌈s/2⌉. Lines 373–376: conjecture vecχ(D)=O(s/ log s) stated (authors note corollary is not optimal).

title: Wheel Classes in Kontsevich Graph Complex and Merkulov's Low-Valence Conjecture
body_tex:
\label{conj:merkulov}
Let $\GC_d^{\mathrm{Me}}$ be the maximal subcomplex of $\GC_d$ spanned by linear combinations of graphs all of whose vertices have valence $3$ or $4$.
Then the inclusion
\[
i \colon \GC_d^{\mathrm{Me}}\hookrightarrow \GC_d
\]
is a quasi-isomorphism.
plain_text: Let $_d^ Me $ be the maximal subcomplex of $_d$ spanned by linear combinations of graphs all of whose vertices have valence $3$ or $4$. Then the inclusion \[ i _d^ Me _d \] is a quasi-isomorphism.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: A central conjecture in Kontsevich graph complex theory: quasi‑isomorphism after restricting to 3‑ and 4‑valent vertices. Resolving it would meaningfully clarify GC_d structure and affect deformation/formality applications.
viability_rationale: Authors verify Merkulov's statement only for wheel classes; full quasi-isomorphism for GC_d requires controlling all graph homology—nontrivial progress but not a near-term closure.
rationale: The paper proves the low-valence property only for wheel classes, not for the entire Kontsevich graph complex inclusion. The full quasi-isomorphism statement remains unproven here.
evidence_snippet: Abstract: 'This verifies that Merkulov's low-valence conjecture holds for the wheel classes.' (paper only treats wheel classes, not full GC_d).

title: Modular Ackermann maps and hierarchical hash constructions
body_tex:
For fixed $m\ge3$ and $k\to\infty$, the map
\[
n \mapsto A_{2^k}(m,n)
\]
approaches the uniform distribution on $\mathbb Z_{2^k}$. In particular, the probability that $A_{2^k}(m,n)$ takes any given value in $\mathbb Z_{2^k}$ converges to $2^{-k}$, up to fluctuations diminishing with $k$.
plain_text: For fixed $m3$ and $k to $, the map \[ n maps to A_ 2^k (m,n) \] approaches the uniform distribution on $ Z_ 2^k $. In particular, the probability that $A_ 2^k (m,n)$ takes any given value in $ Z_ 2^k $ converges to $2^ -k $, up to fluctuations diminishing with $k$.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: Asserts equidistribution for modular Ackermann maps; numerically supported but chiefly a finite combinatorial/dynamical statement with cryptographic motivation and limited broader mathematical depth.
viability_rationale: Numerical evidence supports equidistribution, but nested exponential arithmetic modulo 2^k is delicate; proving full asymptotic uniformity for all k seems challenging within five years.
rationale: Authors present numerical evidence and explicitly state asymptotic equidistribution as a conjecture; no proof or refutation is given in the paper.
evidence_snippet: Lines 184-196: "Numerical experiments... suggest outputs are approximately uniformly distributed... [Conjecture: Asymptotic equidistribution stated]."

title: Plectic Heegner classes
body_tex:
If the character $\chi\colon\G_{\mathfrak{c}}\to\overline{\Q}^\times$ is \emph{primitive}, the following equivalence holds
	\[
	\kappa^{\chi}_{A,\cal{S}}\ \text{ is Selmer}\quad\iff\quad r_\mrm{an}(A/E,\chi)\ge r.
	\]	
    In particular, if $r_\mrm{an}(A/E,\chi)< r$, then $\kappa^{\chi}_{A,\cal{S}}\not=0$.
plain_text: If the character $_ c to ^ x $ is primitive, the following equivalence holds \[ ^ _ A,S \ is Selmer r_an(A/E,) >= r. \] In particular, if $r_an(A/E,)< r$, then $^ _ A,S =0$.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: A Selmer/nonvanishing equivalence for plectic Heegner classes ties analytic rank to Galois cohomology, bearing on BSD-type phenomena and higher‑rank arithmetic—deep within arithmetic geometry.
viability_rationale: Deep Bloch--Kato/BSD-type equivalence for new plectic classes; major arithmetic conjecture with little immediate route to full resolution in five years.
rationale: The equivalence about plectic Heegner classes being Selmer iff analytic rank ≥ r is posed as a conjecture; paper does not prove it.
evidence_snippet: Lines 361-366 (Section 'Conjectures'): conjecture states Selmer ⇔ r_an(A/E,χ) ≥ r; presented without proof.

title: Plectic Heegner classes
body_tex:
If $r_\mrm{alg}(A/E,\chi)\ge r$, then
    \[ 	\kappa^{\chi}_{A,\cal{S}}\not=0\quad\iff\quad r_\mrm{alg}(A/E,\chi)=r.
 	\]	
    Moreover, there exists $w_{A,\cal{S}}^\chi\in \exterior{r} A(L_\frak{c})^\chi$ such that $\kappa^{\chi}_{A,\cal{S}}=\det\big(w_{A,\cal{S}}^\chi\big)$.
plain_text: If $r_alg(A/E,) >= r$, then \[ ^ _ A,S =0 r_alg(A/E,)=r. \] Moreover, there exists $w_ A,S ^ in r A(L_c)^$ such that $^ _ A,S =(w_ A,S ^)$.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: Connects plectic classes to algebraic rank and explicit wedge generators of rational points; refines arithmetic control of Selmer classes, important for explicit BSD-style descriptions.
viability_rationale: Strong arithmetic statement tying classes to rational points; like 1414, this is a deep conjecture in Diophantine/Galois theory with low near-term solvability.
rationale: Paper formulates the algebraic-rank/nonvanishing and determinant description as a conjecture; no proof provided in the text.
evidence_snippet: Lines 384-389: conjecture asserts κ^χ_{A,𝒮} nonzero iff r_alg= r and existence of w with κ^χ_{A,𝒮}=det(w); stated as open.

title: A characterization of the sphere in terms of the stereographic projection
plain_text: Let $K subset E^3$ be a convex body and let $N in K$. If, for all plane $$ such that $ cap K= $ and $N $, there exists a rotation $R^ $ with property that eqnarray R^ (C_N(K_ ))=C_N(K_ ), eqnarray where $K_ := cap K$, then $K$ is an ellipsoid of revolution.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: A geometric rigidity statement characterizing ellipsoids of revolution from axial symmetry of inscribed cones; a natural and structurally meaningful problem in convex geometry.
viability_rationale: Authors obtained related theorems and reduction to regular boundary/axial-symmetry; plausible geometric classification but nontrivial global rigidity remains to be closed.
rationale: The paper proves partial progress (if N is a regular boundary point and cones axially symmetric then K is a body of revolution), but does not fully resolve the stated conjecture that K must be an ellipsoid of revolution.
evidence_snippet: Conjecture: If for all planes Gamma there exists a rotation R^Gamma with R^Gamma(C_N(K_Gamma))=C_N(K_Gamma) then K is an ellipsoid of revolution. Theorem: under N regular boundary point, axial symmetry of inscribed cones implies K is a body of revolution.

title: L-manifolds
body_tex:
There exist a family of spaces (or rather Artin stacks) $\cL_{0,n}$ forming a cyclic operad, and equipped with the ``forgetful'' maps
$p\from \cL_{0,n} \to \cL_{0,m}$, for $m \le n$, as well as the ``section'' maps $x_i\from \cL_{0,n} \to \cL_{0,n+1}$ for $1 \le i \le n+1$, satisfying the obvious relations (as for $\wbar \cM_{0,n}$),
such that the spaces $H^\bullet_{\ubf{n}} \isom H^\bullet(\cL_{0,n})$.
plain_text: There exist a family of spaces (or rather Artin stacks) $_ 0,n $ forming a cyclic operad, and equipped with the ``forgetful'' maps $p _ 0,n to _ 0,m $, for $m <= n$, as well as the ``section'' maps $x_i _ 0,n to _ 0,n+1 $ for $1 <= i <= n+1$, satisfying the obvious relations (as for $ _ 0,n $), such that the spaces $H^_ n H^(_ 0,n )$.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: Building moduli-like Artin stacks forming a cyclic operad would create a deep geometric analogue to Frobenius-manifold theory, linking Lie-infinity structures with moduli geometry — a significant conceptual bridge in the field.
viability_rationale: Constructing Artin stacks with the required operadic and cohomological properties is foundational and technically heavy in algebraic geometry; plausible long-term project but unlikely to be settled imminently.
rationale: The author formulates existence of Artin stacks L_{0,n} with specified properties as a conjecture; the paper develops related theory but does not prove this existence statement.
evidence_snippet: Conjecture (lines 256-260): There exist a family of spaces (Artin stacks) \cL_{0,n} forming a cyclic operad, with maps and H^\bullet(\cL_{0,n}) as stated.

title: Optimizing Riesz means of Robin Laplace operators on cuboids in a semiclassical limit
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: A monotonicity claim for a transition parameter in semiclassical Riesz-mean shape optimization; important inside spectral optimization but narrow and technical in scope.
viability_rationale: Specialized, recent spectral-geometry conjecture with strong two-term asymptotics and evidence in the paper. Remaining is a technical strict-inequality gap—plausibly resolvable but nontrivial within a few years.
rationale: Authors state the inequality β(γ,d) > β_W(γ,d-1) as a conjecture; the paper discusses related asymptotics but does not prove this monotonicity in full generality.
evidence_snippet: Conjecture: For any d≥1 and γ>0, β(γ,d) > β_W(γ,d-1). (Posed in the paper; not proved there.)

title: Beatty solutions of almost Golomb equations
body_tex:
\label{conj:defect}
The gap sequence of~$\mathcal{D}$ on the alphabet
$\{3,4,7\}$ is a coding of the fixed point of a
primitive substitution; equivalently,~$\mathcal{D}$
is a morphic set.
plain_text: The gap sequence of $D$ on the alphabet $ 3,4,7 $ is a coding of the fixed point of a primitive substitution; equivalently, $D$ is a morphic set.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: A narrowly framed combinatorial/automatic-sequence claim: that one specific gap sequence is morphic (a coding of a primitive substitution), of technical interest within symbolic dynamics and combinatorics on words.
viability_rationale: Concrete finite combinatorics-on-words claim; approachable via substitution/automatic-sequence methods and likely resolvable with focused analysis. Moderate chance within five years, given limited global obstacles.
rationale: The statement about the gap sequence on alphabet {3,4,7} being morphic is posed as a conjecture in the paper and is not proved there.
evidence_snippet: Conjecture: The gap sequence of 𝒟 on the alphabet {3,4,7} is a coding of the fixed point of a primitive substitution; equivalently, 𝒟 is a morphic set.

title: Modularity of special cycles on Shimura varieties: a survey
body_tex: The series $\Theta^{2,\vir}_{\A_n}$ is a holomorphic Siegel modular form of weight $2n$ with respect to $\Sp_{4}(\Z)$ valued in $\CH^{2n-1}(\cA_n)$, which vanishes at all cusps.
plain_text: The series $^ 2, _ _n $ is a holomorphic Siegel modular form of weight $2n$ with respect to $_ 4 ()$ valued in $^ 2n-1 (_n)$, which vanishes at all cusps.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: Extends the Kudla program to degree-2 Siegel modularity for virtual higher-codimension cycles on A_n; a structurally meaningful conjecture within arithmetic geometry with clear consequences for cycles and modularity.
viability_rationale: Specific higher-codimension Kudla-type modularity; authors flag excess-intersection difficulties and give no concrete finite reduction. Partial precedents exist but full statement seems technically challenging in short term.
rationale: The paper explicitly poses this as a conjecture in the survey and does not prove it; no resolution is given here.
evidence_snippet: Lines 462-464: "The series Θ^{2,vir}_{A_n} is a holomorphic Siegel modular form ... valued in CH^{2n-1}(A_n), which vanishes at all cusps." (stated as Conjecture)

title: Operator symmetric moduli and sharp triangle inequalities
body_tex:
\label{conj:triangle-ineq-sym-modulus}
		Let $X,Y\in\Mn$. Then there exist unitaries $U,V$ such that
		\[
		|X+Y|_{\mathrm{sym}}\le \sqrt{2}\left(  U|X|_{\mathrm{sym}}U^*+V|Y|_{\mathrm{sym}}V^*\right),
		\]
		and the constant $\sqrt{2}$ is sharp.
plain_text: Let $X,Y in $. Then there exist unitaries $U,V$ such that \[ |X+Y|_ sym <= 2( U|X|_ sym U^*+V|Y|_ sym V^*), \] and the constant $2$ is sharp.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: A concrete operator inequality question within matrix analysis; resolves a delicate gap between symmetric moduli and Thompson-type results. Significant to specialists but limited to a focused subfield.
viability_rationale: Substantial local progress: Thompson-type inequality for qsym proved and conjecture reduces to bounding qsym by sym up to unitary. Plausible route exists but nontrivial technical gap remains.
rationale: Author poses this inequality as a conjecture and discusses it remains delicate; no proof appears in the paper for the exact matrix domination with constant sqrt(2).
evidence_snippet: Lines 185-193: "We conjecture... Let X,Y\in M_n. Then ... |X+Y|_{sym} <= sqrt{2}( U|X|_{sym}U^*+V|Y|_{sym}V^* ), and the constant sqrt{2} is sharp."

title: The Gromov-Hausdorff distance between $l^p$-products of metric spaces
body_tex:
Suppose that for arbitrary sequences of metric spaces
$\{(X_n, d_{X_n}\}_{n \in \N}$, \hfill\break $\{(Y_n, d_{Y_n}\}_{n \in \N}$,
$\sup_{n \in \N} \diam(X_n) < \infty$ and $\sup_{n \in \N} \diam(Y_n) < \infty$.
Then is the estimate
$$d_{GH}^{c} \biggl( \prod_{n=1}^{\infty} X_n, \ \prod_{n=1}^{\infty} Y_n \biggr) \le \sup_{n \in \N} d_{GH}^{c} (X_n, Y_n)$$ of $l^{\infty}$-products correct in the general case?
plain_text: Suppose that for arbitrary sequences of metric spaces $ (X_n, d_ X_n _ n in $, $ (Y_n, d_ Y_n _ n in $, $_ n in (X_n) < $ and $_ n in (Y_n) < $. Then is the estimate $$d_ GH ^ c ( _ n=1 ^ X_n, \ _ n=1 ^ Y_n ) <= _ n in d_ GH ^ c (X_n, Y_n)$$ of $l^ $-products correct in the general case?
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: A natural estimate question for infinite l^infty-products in Gromov–Hausdorff theory; primarily a technical metric-geometry problem with limited broader impact.
viability_rationale: Finite and vanishing-diameter cases are proved; difficulty is nonvanishing tail in l^∞-products. Problem is specialized (not a grand conjecture) and likely approachable with compactness/embedding techniques, so moderate near-term solvability.
rationale: The author poses the l^\infty-product estimate as an open conjecture; only a special case (diameters →0) is proved, not the general statement.
evidence_snippet: Lines 455–456: estimate holds if diameters →0. Lines 459–465: conjecture asking if inequality holds in general.

title: A family of Non-Weierstrass Semigroups
plain_text: If $S$ is satisfies conditions (1) and (2), then it satisfies condition (3) as well; so $S$ is degree-special without further hypotheses.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: A structural claim simplifying degree‑special criteria for numerical semigroups; useful for producing non‑Weierstrass examples but fairly narrow in scope.
viability_rationale: A specific, technical syzygy/semigroup statement accessible to commutative-algebra techniques; authors already supply examples and methods, so experts could likely settle it within a few years. Not a grand longstanding open problem.
rationale: The paper explicitly labels this statement as a conjecture and does not prove it; examples follow but no general proof of (3) from (1),(2) is given.
evidence_snippet: Lines 486–489: Conjecture \label{acyclity} stated. Line 491 onward: examples, not a proof of the conjecture.

title: Solvable compatible Lie algebras with a given nilradical
body_tex: Let $\mathcal{L}$ be a filiform compatible Lie algebra of dimension $n+1$. Then $\mathcal{L}$ is a linear deformation of the algebra $\mathcal{L}_s$ for certain $s \in \mathbb{N}^t$.
plain_text: Let $L$ be a filiform compatible Lie algebra of dimension $n+1$. Then $L$ is a linear deformation of the algebra $L_s$ for certain $s in N^t$.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: A classification-type claim that filiform compatible Lie algebras are linear deformations of model families is a useful structural conjecture in Lie algebra theory, but largely specialist.
viability_rationale: Structural deformation conjecture in Lie-algebra theory; accessible via cohomology/deformation techniques but may need substantial classification work.
rationale: The statement is presented as a Conjecture in the paper (filiform compatible Lie algebra is a linear deformation of L_s) and the authors do not prove it in the nearby text.
evidence_snippet: Lines 900–902: 'Conjecture: Let L be a filiform compatible Lie algebra ... Then L is a linear deformation of the algebra L_s.' No proof given there.

title: Sarnak's Program for Erdős Sieves. Part II: Measure Systems and Applications
body_tex:
\label{cnj: the only one}
					For every Erdős sieve  $R$ with strong light tails for $B_N$, there exists some infinite $A \subset \co_K$ such that $-A+R$ has strong light tails for $B_N$.
plain_text: For every Erdős sieve $R$ with strong light tails for $B_N$, there exists some infinite $A subset _K$ such that $-A+R$ has strong light tails for $B_N$.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: A structural existence claim for shifts making Erdős sieves have strong light tails; a natural generalization of the squarefree case but specialized to sieve/additive structure and ergodic-measure considerations.
viability_rationale: Recent breakthrough (van Doorn–Tao) for squarefree case and authors’ plan to adapt methods suggest plausible progress, but extending to all Erdős sieves and Følner sequence B_N involves nontrivial algebraic/technical obstacles.
rationale: The paper poses the statement as a conjecture and only resolves special cases (e.g. squarefree sieve over Q for I_N); the general statement for B_N is not proved here.
evidence_snippet: “We conjecture that this is not the case. For every Erdős sieve R with strong light tails for B_N, there exists some infinite A ⊂ o_K such that -A+R has strong light tails for B_N.” (lines 1484–1489; follow-up notes show only special-case progress)

title: Toric schemes and integral models for Shimura varieties with $Γ_1(p)$-type level
body_tex:
\label{divconj}
There exists a unique, up to unique isomorphism, pair $({\rm P}_{\CG,\mu}, s_{\CG,\mu})$ of a $\CG$-equivariant $T_{\CG}$-torsor ${\rm P}_{\CG,\mu}\to \Mloc_{\CG,\mu}$ over the local model $\Mloc_{\CG,\mu}$, and a $\CG$-equivariant trivialization (section) $s_{\CG,\mu}$ of this torsor over the generic fiber $\Mloc_{\CG,\mu}\otimes_{O_E}E$, such that
the following condition holds: 

For a  character $\chi: T_{\CG, \br \BZ_p} \to (\BG_m)_{\br\BZ_p}$, consider the 
$(\BG_m)_{O_{\br E}}$-torsor 
\[
{\rm P}_\chi:=(\BG_m)_{O_{\br E}}\times_{\chi, T_\CG} \RP_{\CG,\mu} 
\]
over $\Mloc_{\CG,\mu}\otimes_{O_E}O_{\br E}$, obtained by pushing out ${\rm P}_{\CG,\mu}\otimes_{O_E}O_{\br E}$ by $\chi$. Denote by $\CL_\chi$ the invertible sheaf over 
$\Mloc_{\CG,\mu}\otimes_{O_E}O_{\br E}$ which corresponds to the 
$(\BG_m)_{O_{\br E}}$-torsor ${\rm P}_\chi$. The section $s_{\CG,\mu}$ induces a section $s_\chi$ of the $(\BG_m)_{\br E}$-torsor ${\rm P}_\chi\otimes_{O_{\br E}}\br E$ over $\Mloc_{\CG,\mu}\otimes_{O_E}\breve E$ and, hence, a (meromorphic) section of the  invertible sheaf
$\CL_\chi$ over $\Mloc_{\CG,\mu}\otimes_{O_E}O_{\br E}$. The condition is that, for all such $\chi$, the  divisor of this meromorphic section of $\CL_\chi$ is equal to 
\begin{equation}\label{divsum}
D_{\chi}:=\sum_{W^Kt_{\bar\mu'} W^K, \mu'\in \Lambda_{\{\mu\}}}e\cdot \langle \bar\mu',\chi\rangle\cdot  Z_{\bar\mu'},
\end{equation}
where $\langle\ ,\ \rangle$ is the pairing (\ref{pair2}) and $e$ is the ramification index of $E$ over $\BQ_p$.

interestingness_rationale: Divisor conjecture prescribing canonical T-torsor and exact boundary divisors on local models; a deep structural statement foundational to the geometry of local models and integral-model constructions.
viability_rationale: A technical divisor statement about local models; plausible using advancing local-model technology, but uniqueness and Cartier divisor issues in general remain delicate.
rationale: The divisor conjecture is formulated for general local models. The paper discusses and perhaps verifies special cases later, but the general conjecture remains posed.

title: On a conjecture of Fujino and Sato
plain_text: Let $X$ be a complete $$-factorial toric variety. Then there exists a finite sequence consisting of flips, flops, or anti-flips equation* X=:X_0 X_1 X_m=:X' equation* such that $X'$ is a projective $$-factorial toric variety.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: A structural conjecture in toric birational geometry linking flips/flops to projectivity; resolving it would have substantial consequences for MMP-style factorization in toric (and related) settings.
viability_rationale: Local context indicates the note already provides conceptual proofs and extends to wMDS (existence of a D-flip to a projective MDS), so the toric conjecture appears effectively resolved here.
rationale: Conjecture 1.4 is stated (from Fujino-Sato). The paper gives conceptual proofs of related results and an analogous statement for wMDS, but does not claim to prove the exact conjectural finite-sequence factorization in full generality.
evidence_snippet: Lines 362-368: Conjecture 1.4 states existence of a finite sequence of flips/flops/anti-flips to a projective Q-factorial toric variety; paper discusses related results but not a full proof.

title: Uniformly elliptic boundary value problems
plain_text: Let $$ be a discrete group, and assume for simplicity that $E$ can be realized as a locally compact second-countable metric space. Define the following objects: Let $Pos^ bg,spin _n(E)$ consist of $n$-dimensional spin manifolds equipped with a upsc-metric of bounded geometry and a uniformly cobounded Lipschitz map to $E$, modulo finite-width spin-bordisms equipped with a upsc-metric of bounded geometry and a uniformly cobounded Lipschitz map to $E$. Let $^bg,spin_n(E)$ consist of $n$-dimensional spin manifolds of bounded geometry together with a uniformly cobounded Lipschitz map to $E$, modulo finite-width spin-bordisms of bounded geometry and a uniformly cobounded Lipschitz map to $E$. Let $R^ bg,spin _n(E)$ consist of spin-manifolds with possibly non-empty boundary, equipped with a bounded-geometry metric having upsc on the boundary (if existent) and a uniformly cobounded Lipschitz map to $E$, modulo the bounded-geometry version of the relation for $R^spin_n$. Then, there is a commutative diagram & Pos^bg,spin_ * (E) & _*^bg,spin(E) & R^bg,spin_*(E) & & K_ *+1 (D^*_u(E) ) & K_*^ u (E) & K_*( ell ^ _r ) & with exact rows. Here the uniform index map $K_*^ u (E) to K_*(C^*_u(E)))= K_*( ell ^() _r )$ factors as the composition $$ K^ u _*(E) K^ u _*(E)=K^ _*(E; ell ^()) _ ell ^() K_*( ell ^() _r ) \; , $$ where the first map is induced by the canonical map $E to E$, and the second map is the Baum-Connes assembly map with $ ell ^()$-coefficients. The vertical arrows are given by the push-forwards of the uniform $$-invariant, the uniform K-homology class of the Dirac operator, and the uniform APS-index along the maps uniformly cobounded Lipschitz maps to $E$, respectively.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: Targets a broad framework connecting bounded-geometry positive-scalar-curvature sequences to analytic surgery with ℓ^∞-coefficients and Baum–Connes assembly—central to large-scale index theory and PSC moduli questions.
viability_rationale: Many analytic pieces established and author notes remaining steps (uniform APS-index, geometric exactness) appear reachable; concrete program and partial results make near-term solution plausible.
rationale: The thesis conjectures a commutative exact diagram mapping bounded-geometry positive-scalar-curvature data to uniform analytic K-theory; the author describes parts as unproven and open.

title: Universal Functions for Topological Correlators
body_tex:
\label{conj_GK}
Let $M$ be as above, and $s\in\mathbb Z$. There exist universal functions
\bea\label{Cz Cz12}
Q_s, R_s, T_s, V_s, W_s, X_s  &\in \mathbb C[[z]]~, \\
S_s, S_{1,s}, Y_s, Y_{1,s}, Z_s,  Z_{11,s} &\in \mathbb C[[z^{\frac{1}{2}}]]~,
\eea
such that for any $\alpha\in K(S)$ with $\rk(\alpha)=s$ we have 
{\normalfont
\bea\label{GK conjecture}
\segre_\alpha[p',x']=\universal_\alpha[p',x'] \Big|_{z^{\frac12\vd M}}~,
\eea
}
where 
{\normalfont
\bea\label{universal series}
\universal_\alpha[p',x']& \coloneqq 2^{2-\chih+K^2}V_s^{c_2(\alpha)}W_s^{c_1(\alpha)^2}X_s^{\chih} Y_s^{c_1(\alpha)K}Z_s^{K^2}e^{x'^2 Q_s + c_1(\alpha)x' R_s + x'K S_{s}  +  p'\, T_s} \\
&\quad \times \sum_a (-1)^{a c_1}\, \sw(a) Y_{1,s}^{c_1(\alpha)a}e^{a x' S_{1,s}} Z_{11,s}^{a^2}~,
\eea
}
and 
\bea\label{changeofvar}
z = t (1+(1-\tfrac{s}{2}) t)^{1-\frac{s}{2}}~.
\eea
Here, 
\bea\label{Vs Ws Xs Qs Rs Ts} 
V_s(t) &= (1+(1-\tfrac{s}{2})t)^{2-s} (1+(2-\tfrac{s}{2})t)^s~, \\
W_{s}(t)&= 2\left(2+(2-s)t\right)^{-\frac32+\frac12s}\left(2+(4-s)t\right)^{\frac12-\frac12s}~, \\
X_{s}(t)&= (1+(1-\tfrac{s}{2})t)^{\frac12s^2-\frac54s}(1+(2-\tfrac{s}{2})t)^{-\frac12 s^2+\frac12}(1+(1-\tfrac{s}{2})(2-\tfrac{s}{2})t)^{-\frac12}~, \\
Q_s(t) &= \tfrac{1}{2}t(1+(1-\tfrac{s}{2}) t)~, \quad  R_s(t) = t~, \quad 
T_{s}(t) = 2 t (1+ \tfrac{1}{2}(1-\tfrac{s}{2})(2-\tfrac{s}{2})t)~, \\
\eea
and moreover $Y_s$, $Y_{1,s}$,  $Z_s$, $Z_{11,s}$, $S_s$, $S_{1,s}$ are all algebraic functions.

Here, V_s(t) &= (1+(1-s 2 )t)^ 2-s (1+(2-s 2 )t)^s , W_ s (t)&= 2(2+(2-s)t)^ -32+12s (2+(4-s)t)^ 12-12s , X_ s (t)&= (1+(1-s 2 )t)^ 12s^2-54s (1+(2-s 2 )t)^ -12 s^2+12 (1+(1-s 2 )(2-s 2 )t)^ -12 , Q_s(t) &= 1 2 t(1+(1-s 2 ) t) , R_s(t) = t , T_ s (t) = 2 t (1+ 1 2 (1-s 2 )(2-s 2 )t) , and moreover $Y_s$, $Y_ 1,s $, $Z_s$, $Z_ 11,s $, $S_s$, $S_ 1,s $ are all algebraic functions.

interestingness_rationale: Göttsche–Kool conjecture proposes universal generating functions for Segre invariants, tying Seiberg–Witten/u‑plane geometry to sheaf moduli—deep and influential within enumerative geometry and gauge/physics connections, but specialized.
viability_rationale: Göttsche--Kool conjecture has substantial supporting checks and explicit formulae for small ranks; full universal-series proof is technical and challenging, so modest near-term likelihood.
rationale: The Göttsche–Kool universal-functions conjecture is stated as a conjecture. The paper discusses and verifies cases but does not claim a full proof of the general conjecture.
evidence_snippet: Lines 1046–1078: "Let M be as above... There exist universal functions ... such that ... (Göttsche–Kool conjecture)"; subsequent text gives candidate functions and partial checks.

title: Polytopes of alternating sign matrices with dihedral-subgroup symmetry
plain_text: Let $n >= 5$ be such that $n 2 4$, and set $k = n/2$. Then the dimension of $P_$ is $ (n-1)^2 4 - 2$.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: Nontrivial structural conjecture giving an explicit dimension formula for the quarter-turn symmetric ASM polytope; significant within polyhedral combinatorics and ASM classification but of specialized scope beyond the subfield.
viability_rationale: Authors resolved other symmetry classes with explicit polyhedral tools; only quarter-turn case remains with parity-type CG constraints. The gap is technical and narrowly focused, making a proof likely within a few years.
rationale: The paper states this dimension formula as Conjecture~\ref{conj:qtsasm:dim} for QTSASM and no proof appears nearby; it remains posed as an open question.
evidence_snippet: Conjecture: Let n≥5 with n≠2 (mod 4). Then dim P_QTSASM = ⌊(n-1)^2/4⌋ - 2. (lines 3266–3269)

title: Algebraic $K$-theory of stably compact spaces
body_tex:
\label{ktheoryrightkan}
The universal finitary localizing invariant $\mathcal{U} :  \CAlg(\PR^L_{\dual}) \rightarrow \CAlg(\mathrm{Mot})$ is right Kan extended from its restriction to $ \CAlg(\PR^L_{\st,\omega}).$
plain_text: The universal finitary localizing invariant $U : (^L_ ) (Mot)$ is right Kan extended from its restriction to $ (^L_ , ).$
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: A structural monoidal right-Kan extension claim for the universal finitary localizing invariant would streamline K-theory constructions and has significant categorical consequences beyond a single computation.
viability_rationale: A non-monoidal analogue is proved by Efimov; the conjecture is a technical symmetric-monoidal strengthening. Given close prior result and concrete categorical methods, a resolution within five years is moderately likely.
rationale: The claim is explicitly posed as a conjecture in the paper; authors note the non-monoidal analogue is known (Efimov) but do not prove the monoidal right Kan extension statement here.
evidence_snippet: Lines 417–421: 'We originally hoped ... the following claim:' then Conjecture №ktheoryrightkan; Efimov gives the analogous non-monoidal statement.

title: Dynamics of Nesterov's Accelerated Gradient Descent in Quadratic Games
body_tex:
\label{conj:local}
Under the same setup as Theorem~\ref{thm:instability_smooth}, if all eigenvalues of $G$ are in $\R_{> 0}$, the NAGD dynamics $\ddot{x} + \frac{3}{t}\dot{x} + F(x) = 0$ are locally asymptotically stable at $x^\star$, for each fixed $t_0 > 0$.
plain_text: Under the same setup as Theorem , if all eigenvalues of $G$ are in $_ > 0 $, the NAGD dynamics $x + 3 t x + F(x) = 0$ are locally asymptotically stable at $x^$, for each fixed $t_0 > 0$.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: Fills the stability side of a stability/instability dichotomy for Nesterov dynamics in smooth games; important for algorithmic dynamics but specialized to this momentum ODE setting.
viability_rationale: Authors give a clear perturbation roadmap and Bessel-based decay estimates; the remaining technical step is deriving explicit transition-matrix bounds. Local, technical gap likely resolvable within a few years.
rationale: The paper formulates local asymptotic stability as a conjecture for smooth games; only a justification sketch is given and a full rigorous proof is deferred.
evidence_snippet: Section: 'the stability direction remains conjectural...' and Conjecture (Local stability for smooth games): if eigenvalues in R_{>0}, NAGD is locally asymptotically stable (lines 522–535, 536–541).

title: Fano varieties with split tangent sheaf
plain_text: Let $X$ be a projective manifold such that $T_X=V_1 V_2$. Assume that $X$ is rationally connected. Then $V_1$ and $V_2$ are integrable.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: A Beauville-type integrability conjecture in algebraic geometry: resolving it yields structural product-decomposition consequences for rationally connected manifolds and impacts MMP and foliation theory.
viability_rationale: Active field with substantial partial results and concrete MMP/induction strategy; remaining global integrability steps nontrivial but plausibly closable within a few years.
rationale: Conjecture is posed for all rationally connected projective manifolds. The paper proves integrability in (mildly singular) Fano cases but does not settle the full smooth/rationally connected statement.
evidence_snippet: Lines 458-461: conjecture stated. Lines 463-471: paper cites partial results and discusses MMP strategy; proves cases for Fano/mildly singular varieties rather than the full conjecture.

title: Irreducible distinguishing colourings and the Axiom of Choice
body_tex:
The following statements are equivalent the Axiom of Choice in ZF assuming the definition of a cardinal number as  an equivalence class of the equinumerous relation.
\begin{enumerate}[label=\textnormal{\arabic*)}]
    \item Every infinite connected graph has a chromatic index.
    \item Every infinite connected graph has a distinguishing chromatic number.
    \item Every infinite connected graph has a distinguishing chromatic index.
    \item Every infinite connected graph has a distinguishing number.
    \item Every infinite connected graph has a distinguishing chromatic index.
\end{enumerate}
plain_text: The following statements are equivalent the Axiom of Choice in ZF assuming the definition of a cardinal number as an equivalence class of the equinumerous relation. [label=)] Every infinite connected graph has a chromatic index. Every infinite connected graph has a distinguishing chromatic number. Every infinite connected graph has a distinguishing chromatic index. Every infinite connected graph has a distinguishing number. Every infinite connected graph has a distinguishing chromatic index.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: Proposes equivalence of natural infinite-graph colouring/existence statements to the Axiom of Choice; a resolution would have broad foundational consequences linking combinatorics and set theory.
viability_rationale: Equivalences to the Axiom of Choice are classic set-theoretic questions; resolution likely requires model/independence techniques—feasible but may be delicate, giving modest near-term solvability odds.
rationale: Authors explicitly mark the question as still open and state the equivalence-to-Choice list as a conjecture; no proof within the paper.
evidence_snippet: Lines 127–136: 'Furthermore, the question mentioned below is still open to the best of our knowledge. Conj: The following statements are equivalent [to AC in ZF]: (1) Every infinite connected graph has a chromatic index; ... (5) Every infinite connected graph has a distinguishing chromatic index.'

title: On the equivariant triangulation of some small covers
body_tex:
\cite{Lutz16vertex}\label{RP4conjecture}
   The minimal triangulation of $\RP^4$ is unique with 16-vertices.
plain_text: The minimal triangulation of $^4$ is unique with 16-vertices.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: Finite combinatorial classification: uniqueness of Lutz's 16-vertex RP^4 triangulation; important within triangulation theory but narrow in scope and largely technical.
viability_rationale: Finite, low-dimensional combinatorial classification (RP^4); prior constructions and computational approaches exist, so a computer-assisted complete classification or uniqueness proof is plausible within five years.
rationale: The paper cites Lutz's conjecture and does not prove or refute it; authors discuss related constructions but do not settle uniqueness of the 16-vertex RP^4 triangulation.
evidence_snippet: Lutz constructed a 16-vertex minimal triangulation of RP^4 and conjectured the minimal triangulation of RP^4 is unique with 16 vertices (Conjecture stated, not resolved in this paper).

title: Khovanov concordance minima and the (4,5) torus knot
body_tex:
\label{conj:algsliceribbon}
	Each concordance class admits a global minimum with respect to $\leq_{H_*}$.
plain_text: Each concordance class admits a global minimum with respect to $_ H_* $.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: Algebraic analogue of the slice–ribbon/global-minimum problem for knot concordance via knot homology inclusions; resolving it would have clear structural consequences in concordance and knot homology theory.
viability_rationale: A strong algebraic analogue of the slice–ribbon/global-minimum problem; long-standing and broad. Only special cases are known (torus cases), no clear route to full generality in five years.
rationale: The paper states this as an open conjecture and only verifies special cases (e.g. Theorem: (4,5) torus knot is a Kh-global minimum); it does not prove the conjecture in general.
evidence_snippet: Lines 221-225: 'Each concordance class admits a global minimum with respect to \leq_{H_*}.' Theorem 242-245 proves only the (4,5) torus knot case.

title: Base change fundamental lemma for Bernstein centers of principal series blocks
body_tex:
\label{conjecture}
        If $\phi\in\mathcal{H}(G_r)$ and $f\in\mathcal{H}(G)$ are associated, then $Z_r*\phi$ and $b_r'(Z_r)*\phi$ are associated for any $Z_r\in \mathfrak{Z}^{\textnormal{st}}(G_r)$.
plain_text: If $ in H(G_r)$ and $f in H(G)$ are associated, then $Z_r*$ and $b_r'(Z_r)*$ are associated for any $Z_r in Z^ st (G_r)$.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: Structural conjecture about base change for the stable Bernstein center; resolving it advances twisted endoscopic transfer and test functions for Shimura varieties, with substantial consequences in representation theory and automorphic forms.
viability_rationale: Several major special cases proved (spherical, parahoric, depth-zero principal series, GL_n) and this paper advances principal-series cases; active progress makes a resolution in five years plausible but nontrivial.
rationale: This is Haines's general conjecture. The paper proves cases for principal-series blocks but does not claim the full general statement, so the exact conjecture as stated remains open.
evidence_snippet: “T. Haines proposed a conjecture ... We have the following conjecture due to T. Haines: If φ∈H(G_r) and f∈H(G) are associated, then Z_r*φ and b_r'(Z_r)*φ are associated for any Z_r∈Z^{st}(G_r).”

title: Entropy formula for surface diffeomorphisms
body_tex:
For any $C^r$ ($r>1$) diffeomorphism $f$ on a compact surface $M$ with $h_{\rm top}(f)>\frac{\lambda^{+}(f)}{r}$. For any $0<\delta< h_{\rm top}(f)$, $$h_{\rm top}(f)=\limsup_{n\to+\infty}\frac{1}{n}\log \#\mathcal{P}^{n}_{\delta}$$ where $\mathcal{P}^{n}_{\delta}$ is the set of $n$-periodic points with Lyapunov
	exponents $\delta$-away from zero.
plain_text: For any $C^r$ ($r>1$) diffeomorphism $f$ on a compact surface $M$ with $h_ top (f)> ^ + (f) r $. For any $0<< h_ top (f)$, $$h_ top (f)=_ n to + 1 n \#P^ n _ $$ where $P^ n _ $ is the set of $n$-periodic points with Lyapunov exponents $$-away from zero.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: A central problem in smooth surface dynamics linking topological entropy to growth of hyperbolic periodic points in the C^r setting; known in C^∞, with substantial structural consequences if resolved.
viability_rationale: C^∞ case is known and recent advances address related invariants; the C^r conjecture is delicate but active work gives a moderate chance of resolution in five years.
rationale: The paper reproduces Burguet's conjecture about growth of hyperbolic periodic points and does not claim to prove this exact statement.
evidence_snippet: Lines 188–191 present the conjecture (cited to Burguet); surrounding text discusses related results but does not prove this claim.

title: The minimum spectral radius of $tP_4$-saturated graphs

For $n$ sufficiently large, $$sat(n, tP_4)=cases n+12t-12 2 , &if $n$ is even; n+12t-11 2 , & if $n$ is odd. cases$$ Moreover, $(t-1)N_4 cup 1 2 (n-12t+12)P_2 in SAT(n, tP_4) $ if $n$ is even and $N^*_4 cup (t-2)N_4 cup 1 2 (n-12t+11)P_2 in SAT(n, tP_4)$ if $n$ is odd, where $N_4$ and $N_4^*$ are shown in Figure .

interestingness_rationale: Exact saturation numbers and extremal constructions for tP4 are a concrete, longstanding problem in extremal graph theory; significant within the subfield but limited broader impact.
viability_rationale: Combinatorial saturation conjecture from 2015 with several confirmed cases (t=2, etc.). Plausible progress by extremal methods, but full general resolution requires substantial casework.
rationale: This is a prior conjecture (CFFGJM2015) on sat(n,tP4). The paper cites partial confirmations (t=2) but does not claim to prove the general statement, so it remains unresolved here.
evidence_snippet: Lines 95–106: The authors restate Conjecture 1.1 from CFFGJM2015, note it was confirmed for t=2 and n≥12, but do not prove the general t≥2 case in this paper.

title: Quadratic irrational analogues of Ramanujan's series for $1/π$
body_tex:

The remaining $N$ in Table~\ref{T:1.2} are $N\in\left\{15,35,51,99,187,403\right\}$. What can be said about them? {\color{blue}{See Theorem~\ref{Th:11.1} for $N=187$.}}

title: An elementary approach to non-symmetric shift operators and their q-analogs
body_tex:
\label{conj:fundamental_shift_operator}
    For any linear character $\eps$ of $W$, there exists a non-symmetric shift operator $\tilde{\calG}^{(\eps)}_\pm(k)$ with shift $\pm l$ that is fundamental in the following sense. All non-symmetric shift operators with shift $\pm l$ are of the form
    \[
        \tilde{\calG}^{(\eps)}_\pm(k)T_p(k) \qquad \text{for some } p \in S(\frakh).
    \]
    In particular, the non-symmetric shift operator $\calG^{(\eps)}_\pm(k)$ decomposes as follows:
    \[
        \calG^{(\eps)}_\pm(k) = \tilde{\calG}^{(\eps)}_\pm(k) \prod_{\alpha \in R^0_+, \thinspace l(\alpha) = 0}(T_{\alpha^\vee}(k) - k^0(\alpha)).
    \]
    Observe that $\calG^{(\eps)}_\pm(k) = \tilde{\calG}^{(\eps)}_\pm(k)$ for the sign character, so that the conjecture states that $\calG^{(\eps)}_\pm(k)$ is fundamental in this case.

All non-symmetric shift operators with shift $ l$ are of the form \[ ^ () _(k)T_p(k) for some p in S(). \] In particular, the non-symmetric shift operator $^ () _(k)$ decomposes as follows: \[ ^ () _(k) = ^ () _(k) _ in R^0_+, l() = 0 (T_ ^ (k) - k^0()). \] Observe that $^ () _(k) = ^ () _(k)$ for the sign character, so that the conjecture states that $^ () _(k)$ is fundamental in this case.

interestingness_rationale: Structural existence/fundamental-factorization claim for non-symmetric shift operators; would clarify classification and restriction phenomena in Dunkl/Cherednik operator theory. Evidence: holds in BC1 and symmetric integral-multiplicity cases.

title: An elementary approach to non-symmetric shift operators and their q-analogs
body_tex:
\label{conj:fundamental_shift_operator_q}
    For any linear character $\eps$ of $W_0$, there exists a non-symmetric $q$-shift operator $\tilde{\calG}^{(\eps)}_\pm(k)$ with shift $\pm l$ such that
    \[
         \tilde{\calG}^{(\eps)}_\pm(k)E_\mu(k) = \tilde{\calH}^{(\eps)}_\pm(\mu,k)E_{\mu_{\eps,\pm}}(k \pm l^\wedge), \qquad \mu \in L,
    \]

    \[

            \tilde{\calH}^{(\eps)}_\pm(\mu, k) &= \eps(\overline{v}(\mu))\tau_{\overline{v}(\mu),l^\wedge}^\mp\tau_{w_0,l^\wedge}^\pm q^{n_{\eps,k}^\pm}\delta'_\eps(r_{k'}(\mu))\bfc_{\eps_\pm k'}^-(\overline{v}(\mu)w_{0\lambda})(\lambda + \rho_{k'}) \\
            &\qquad \times \bfc_{-\eps_\mp(k' \pm {l^\wedge}')}^+(\overline{v}(\mu)w_{0\lambda})(\lambda + \rho_{k'}).
        \end{aligned}
    \]
    The shift factor $\tilde{\calH}^{(\eps)}_\pm(\mu, k)$ is obtained from $\calH^{(\eps)}_\pm(\mu, k)$ by substituting $\delta'_\eps = \delta'_{\eps,0}$ for $\varpi'$.

\label{conj:TVS}
	Take two independent $\CLE_3$ in a simply connected domain $D$. Let $\g$ be the outer boundary of a single cluster in the union of both $\CLE_3$, i.e. $\g$ is a loop of $\CLE_4$. title: The role of p_1-structures in 3-dimensional Chern-Simons theories
body_tex:
\label{thm:14}
 \ 
 \begin{enumerate}[label=\textnormal{(\arabic*)}]

 \item The Lagrangian~\eqref{eq:28} determines a family of Wick-rotated field
theories. 

 \item The singular limit $e\to \infty $ exists and defines a field theory 
  \begin{equation}\label{eq:29}
     \Fl\:\Btt(\sFRw)\longrightarrow \tV. 
  \end{equation}

 \item The projectivization~$\bFl$ factors through~$\Btt(\sFw)$, hence it is a
projective \emph{topological} field theory.

 \item The projectivity (anomaly)~$\al$ of~$\bFl$ extends to a 4-dimensional
invertible field theory~$\tal$ of oriented manifolds whose partition function
on a closed oriented 4-manifold~$W$ is
  \begin{equation}\label{eq:61}
     \tal(W) = \exp\left( \frac{2\pi ic(\lambda )}{24}\,\langle p_1(W),[W]
     \rangle\right) , 
  \end{equation}
where $c=c(\lambda )\in \QQ$ is the \emph{central charge} associated to the
level~$\lambda $. 

 \end{enumerate}

The singular limit $e to $ exists and defines a field theory equation \:() . equation The projectivization $$ factors through $()$, hence it is a projective topological field theory. The projectivity (anomaly) $$ of $$ extends to a 4-dimensional invertible field theory $$ of oriented manifolds whose partition function on a closed oriented 4-manifold $W$ is equation (W) = ( 2 ic( ) 24 \, p_1(W),[W] ) , equation where $c=c( ) in $ is the central charge associated to the level $ $.

title: The GW/PT conjectures for toric pairs
body_tex:
\label{conj: gw/pt}
Fix a curve class $\beta$, partitions $\bm\mu$, and an insertion $\upalpha$.

    \begin{enumerate}[(i)]
\item The PT series $Z_{\sf PT}(Y|\partial Y;q|\upalpha|\bm\mu)$ is the Laurent expansion of a rational function.
\item Under the change of variables $q = -e^{iu}$, there is an equality of generating functions:
\[
(-q)^{-d_\beta/2}\cdot Z_{\sf PT}(Y|\partial Y;q|\upalpha|\bm\mu)_\beta = (-iu)^{d_\beta+\sum \ell(\mu_j)-|\mu_j|} \cdot Z_{\sf GW}(Y|\partial Y;u|\upalpha|\bm\mu)_\beta.
\]
    \end{enumerate}
plain_text: Fix a curve class $$, partitions $$, and an insertion $$. [(i)] The PT series $Z_ PT (Y| Y;q||)$ is the Laurent expansion of a rational function. Under the change of variables $q = -e^ iu $, there is an equality of generating functions: \[ (-q)^ -d_/2 * Z_ PT (Y| Y;q||)_ = (-iu)^ d_+ ell (_j)-|_j| * Z_ GW (Y| Y;u||)_. \]
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: A deep GW/PT correspondence in the logarithmic (fully singular divisor) setting; solving it yields significant structural bridges in enumerative algebraic geometry and advances understanding of curve-counting theories.
viability_rationale: General GW/PT correspondence is a deep, high-profile conjecture in enumerative geometry; substantial progress exists but full general resolution in five years is unlikely.
rationale: The conjecture is the general GW/PT correspondence; the paper proves it for toric pairs but not in full generality, so the exact general statement remains unresolved here.
evidence_snippet: Lines 713–725 state the general conjecture; paper then explains it will prove the correspondence in the toric case (starting point Theorem C).

For the class of connected chordal graphs on $n\geq 3$ vertices, we have
    $$\RV_{chordal}(n)=\left\{(r,v)\in \mathbb{N}^{2} \mid 1\leq r<\frac{n}{2}, 1\leq v\leq r-\left\lceil\frac{r}{n-2r}\right\rceil+1\right\}.$$

title: Stability conditions on noncommutative crepant resolutions of 3-dimensional isolated singularities

title: Coarse separation and splittings in right-angled Artin groups
body_tex: Let $\Gamma$ be a finite graph and $\mathcal{G}$ a collection of infinite finitely generated virtually nilpotent groups. The graph product $\Gamma \mathcal{G}$ is coarsely separable by a family of subexponential growth if and only if $\Gamma$ is complete or separated by a complete subgraph.

interestingness_rationale: Natural extension of the RAAG coarse-separation characterization to graph products of virtually nilpotent groups; meaningful within geometric group theory with clear structural consequences, but specialised in scope.
viability_rationale: Natural generalisation of proven RAAG result but depends on extending relatively hyperbolic/ thickness tools; plausible but requires nontrivial technical work.
rationale: The authors pose this generalization to graph products as a conjecture; they prove the RAAG case but do not prove this broader statement here.
evidence_snippet: "On the one hand, we expect that: Let Γ be a finite graph and G a collection... The graph product ΓG is coarsely separable..." (lines 236–240).

title: Cyclotomic generating functions, empty weighted complete intersections and positivity

interestingness_rationale: A combinatorial-positivity conjecture (Stanton) about cyclotomic generating functions; meaningful within algebraic combinatorics but of limited cross-field reach despite connections to weighted projective geometry.
viability_rationale: Paper reports substantial partial results (most cases proved) and combinatorial/structural reductions; remaining gaps seem technical rather than deep, making a full resolution plausible within five years though nontrivial work remains.
rationale: The Stanton 'fake Gaussian' conjecture is cited and the authors state they prove most cases only; the full conjecture is not claimed proved.
evidence_snippet: Conjecture (Stanton): If fraction is a polynomial for all m and a is symmetric, then it has non-negative integer coefficients. The paper proves most cases of an unpublished conjecture by Stanton.

title: Cyclotomic generating functions, empty weighted complete intersections and positivity

viability_rationale: Authors and predecessors have proved many special cases and this paper provides new sufficient combinatorial criteria; full generality remains open, so a resolution in five years is plausible but far from certain.

title: Ties in function field prime race

    \]

interestingness_rationale: Algebraic-geometric claim about intersections of constrained secant spans with VP varieties controlling the general fiber of a PGCNN map; meaningful for parametrization theory but narrowly scoped.
viability_rationale: A specific algebraic-geometric statement about generic secant intersections with supporting computations; likely approachable by established algebraic-geometry techniques and the authors' induction framework.
rationale: The conjecture on intersection of constrained secant lines with VP variety is stated and used conditionally; the paper does not prove it, only derives conditional results.
evidence_snippet: Lines 894–899: Conjecture that given general filters the intersection equals the listed vectors; Theorem 1 is conditional on this conjecture.

title: On flat shadow boundaries from point light sources and the characterization of ellipsoids
body_tex:
{Gonzalez_Garcia2022}, \cite[Conjecture~1]{https://doi.org/10.1112/mtk.12176}, \cite[Conjecture~3.1]{flatgrazesconvexbodies}}]\thlabel{con:01}
Let $K\subset\mathbb R^n$, $n\geq 3$, be a convex body and let $S$ be a hypersurface, which is the image of an embedding of the sphere $\mathbb S^{n-1}$, such that $K$ is contained in the interior of $S$. If any point light source on $S$ creates a flat shadow boundary on $K$, then $K$ is an ellipsoid.
plain_text: Gonzalez_Garcia2022 , , ]con:01 Let $K subset R^n$, $n >= 3$, be a convex body and let $S$ be a hypersurface, which is the image of an embedding of the sphere $ S^ n-1 $, such that $K$ is contained in the interior of $S$. If any point light source on $S$ creates a flat shadow boundary on $K$, then $K$ is an ellipsoid.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: A natural extension of Blaschke-type characterizations: verifying that flat shadows from enclosing hypersurfaces force ellipsoids; an appealing geometric problem with classical roots.
viability_rationale: The paper itself proves the conjecture for sufficiently smooth boundaries (and stronger variants), so the stated claim is essentially resolved in this work.
rationale: The point-light-source flat-shadow conjecture is posed historically; the paper confirms it under C^3 (or higher) smoothness but not in full generality, so the exact original conjecture remains open.
evidence_snippet: Lines 141–153: Conjecture stated; paper proves Theorem 1 showing the result for convex bodies with C^3 boundary, not the full conjecture without smoothness.

title: On flat shadow boundaries from point light sources and the characterization of ellipsoids
body_tex:
{Bianchi1987}}]\thlabel{con:02}
Let $K\subset\mathbb R^n$, $n\geq 3$, be a strictly convex body and let $\mathcal S$ be a family of hyperplanes, which is the image of an embedding of the tangent bundle of the sphere $\mathbb S^{n-1}$, such that every hyperplane in $\mathcal S$ intersects the interior of $K$. If any such intersection is centrally symmetric and (with, possibly, a few exceptions) does not contain a possibly existing center of symmetry of $K$, then $K$ is an ellipsoid.
plain_text: Bianchi1987 ]con:02 Let $K subset R^n$, $n >= 3$, be a strictly convex body and let $ S$ be a family of hyperplanes, which is the image of an embedding of the tangent bundle of the sphere $ S^ n-1 $, such that every hyperplane in $ S$ intersects the interior of $K$. If any such intersection is centrally symmetric and (with, possibly, a few exceptions) does not contain a possibly existing center of symmetry of $K$, then $K$ is an ellipsoid.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: Bianchi-style conjecture characterizing ellipsoids via centrally symmetric affine sections; a longstanding, central question in convex geometry with strong duality connections.
viability_rationale: A classical characterization-of-ellipsoids problem with partial dimension-specific results; progress likely incremental, full general resolution in five years is uncertain but possible in special cases.
rationale: Bianchi-type conjecture on centrally symmetric hyperplane sections is discussed; the paper records partial results and known special cases but does not settle the general conjecture.
evidence_snippet: Lines 200–204: Conjecture (cf. Bianchi1987) stated; subsequent text records partial verifications (dimension 3, special families) but not a full proof.

title: Superelliptic Affine Lie algebras and orthogonal polynomials II
body_tex:
\label{conj:kcrit}
For each superelliptic algebra with parameters $(r,m)$, the critical level is
\[
k_{\mathrm{crit}}(r,m) = -h^{\vee} + f\!\left(\frac{r}{2}\!\left(1+\frac{1}{m}\right)\right),
\]
where $h^\vee$ is the dual Coxeter number of $\mathfrak{g}$ and $f$ is an explicit rational
function. In particular, for the DJKM case $(r,m)=(1,2)$, this recovers the classical
critical level $k_{\mathrm{crit}}=-h^{\vee}$.
plain_text: For each superelliptic algebra with parameters $(r,m)$, the critical level is \[ k_ crit (r,m) = -h^ + f\!(r 2 \!(1+1 m )), \] where $h^$ is the dual Coxeter number of $g$ and $f$ is an explicit rational function. In particular, for the DJKM case $(r,m)=(1,2)$, this recovers the classical critical level $k_ crit =-h^ $.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: Gives a structural formula for critical levels in superelliptic vertex-algebra families; resolving it clarifies reducibility loci of Wakimoto modules and impacts representation-theoretic structure.
viability_rationale: Predicts an explicit rational correction to critical levels in vertex-algebra theory; representation-theoretic difficulties make a near-term proof uncertain.
rationale: In the Outlook the authors propose a formula for the critical level k_crit(r,m) depending on an explicit rational function f; this is stated as a conjecture and not proved in the paper.
evidence_snippet: Lines 902–909: Conjecture states k_{crit}(r,m) = -h^vee + f(r/2(1+1/m)) and cites the DJKM case, presented without proof.

title: The Drinfeld Center of the Generic Temperley--Lieb Category
body_tex:
There exists a $(P/Q)^{\oplus 3}$ bicharacter $\gamma$ such that the canonical functor $$Z:\bigg(U_q(\mathfrak g)\mathbf{-mod}\boxtimes U_q(\mathfrak g)\mathbf{-mod}^\rev \boxtimes \Rep{P/Q} \bigg)^\gamma \to \Dr{U_q(\mathfrak g)\mathbf{-mod}}$$
	is equivalences of braided tensor categories whenever $q\in\CC$ is not a root of unity.
plain_text: There exists a $(P/Q)^ 3 $ bicharacter $$ such that the canonical functor $$Z:(U_q( g)-mod U_q( g)-mod^ P/Q )^ to U_q( g)-mod $$ is equivalences of braided tensor categories whenever $q in $ is not a root of unity.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: Extends the Drinfeld-center description to general quantum group types via a bicharacter twist; impactful for tensor-category descriptions of U_q(g)-mod, though technical and Lie-type specific.
viability_rationale: Author indicates ultraproduct techniques extend to other Lie types; complications from affine Weyl filtrations remain but resolution seems moderately plausible.
rationale: In 'Other Lie Types' the author conjectures existence of a bicharacter making a canonical functor an equivalence for U_q(g)-mod when q is not a root of unity; this is left as open future work.
evidence_snippet: Lines 2017–2019: Conjecture asserts existence of a (P/Q)^{⊕3} bicharacter γ giving an equivalence of braided tensor categories for non-root-of-unity q.

title: Tempered vs generic automorphic functions and the canonical filtration on automorphic functions

viability_rationale: This is a form of Arthur's global decomposition—a deep, longstanding conjecture. Only special cases are known (e.g. Kazhdan–Okounkov); no concrete path to full resolution is presented, so near-term solution unlikely.

title: Derived Weil Representation and Relative Langlands Duality
plain_text: We have the equivalence of categories equation* _^(S), equation* and the equivalence is compatible with the action of derived Hecke categories of $ G $ and $ H $ on both side.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: Asserts a categorical equivalence identifying the Weil category with QCoh on a Slodowy slice, compatible with Hecke actions. A substantial structural claim in relative Langlands/representation theory with notable consequences; partial GL results exist.
viability_rationale: GL(n),GL(m) case proved; derived Satake and Slodowy-slice techniques available. Extending to other classical dual pairs is plausible but technically demanding.
rationale: The paper explicitly conjectures the categorical equivalence and compatibility; it proves the GL_n,GL_m case but not the full family (other classical groups) asserted by the conjecture.
evidence_snippet: Conjecture stated (lines 217-223). The GL_n,GL_m case is proved in this paper (lines 231-234).

title: Differential graded categories in holomorphic symplectic geometry
body_tex: Let $(\mr{X},\sigma)$ be a holomorphic symplectic manifold and let $\mathfrak{L}$ be a (countable) collection of orientable compact Lagrangian submanifolds such that the weight filtration of $\mr{H}\mathcal{DQ}_\mathfrak{L}(\mr{X},\sigma)$ is pure. Then the differential graded category $\mathcal{DQ}_\mathfrak{L}(\mr{X},\sigma)$ is formal.
plain_text: Let $(X,)$ be a holomorphic symplectic manifold and let $L$ be a (countable) collection of orientable compact Lagrangian submanifolds such that the weight filtration of $HDQ_L(X,)$ is pure. Then the differential graded category $DQ_L(X,)$ is formal.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: Purity-implies-formality statement for deformation-quantisation dg categories; meaningful within holomorphic symplectic/mirror-symmetry contexts and could simplify categorical analyses if true.
viability_rationale: Author proves many local cases and shows morphism spaces carry mixed Hodge structures; extending 'purity implies formality' seems plausible via existing deformation-quantisation/Hodge techniques but nontrivial to complete generally.
rationale: The paper states 'purity implies formality' as a conjecture (after proving formality in special Kähler cases); the general purity-implies-formality statement is posed but not proved here.
evidence_snippet: Lines 190-193: "In fact, the morphism spaces ... Following the philosophy that 'purity implies formality', we expect the next to be true: \begin{conjecture} ... Then the differential graded category DQ_L(X,sigma) is formal. \end{conjecture}"

title: Stably tangential strict hyperbolization
body_tex:
\label{conj:nonzero-pi-orientable}
For the orientable flat manifolds $\widehat{\msf{LS}}_n$, the mod $2$ reduction of the $i$-th Pontryagin class $p_i(\widehat{\msf{LS}}_n)$ is non-zero, provided $n\geq 8i$.
plain_text: For the orientable flat manifolds $ LS _n$, the mod $2$ reduction of the $i$-th Pontryagin class $p_i( LS _n)$ is non-zero, provided $n >= 8i$.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: A concrete nonvanishing statement about mod‑2 Pontryagin classes for an explicit family of flat manifolds: useful for producing examples, but narrow in scope and largely example-driven.
viability_rationale: Authors already prove low-index cases (p1,p2) and give explicit constructions; extending to general i seems approachable by characteristic-class/topological methods.
rationale: Authors prove several low-degree instances (p1, p2 nonzero for specific n) but explicitly state the general mod 2 nonvanishing conjecture for all n≥8i as a conjecture, not proved.
evidence_snippet: Lines 1945-1947: p1,p2 nonzero for certain n. Lines 1963-1965: conjecture asserting mod 2 reduction of p_i nonzero for n≥8i.

title: Stability of syzygy bundles of Ulrich bundles
body_tex:
Let $X$ be a smooth irreducible projective variety  with a fixed ample
line bundle $\cO _X (1)$ and let $\cE$ be an Ulrich  bundle on $X$. There exists an integer $m¨_0\gg 0$ such that for all $m\ge m_0$ the syzygy bundle $S_{\cE(m)}$ associated to $\cE(m)$ is semistable.
plain_text: Let $X$ be a smooth irreducible projective variety with a fixed ample line bundle $ _X (1)$ and let $$ be an Ulrich bundle on $X$. There exists an integer $m¨_0 0$ such that for all $m >= m_0$ the syzygy bundle $S_ (m) $ associated to $(m)$ is semistable.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: A natural semistability-after-twist statement for syzygy bundles of Ulrich bundles; meaningful within vector‑bundle/moduli theory but specialist and technically focused.
viability_rationale: Plausible heuristic and examples show twisting helps, but the conjecture is very general across all projective varieties; likely technically challenging to settle universally soon.
rationale: Paper proves semistability in many cases and gives evidence, but presents the general statement about syzygy bundles of Ulrich bundles after twists as a conjecture/guess; not proved in full generality here.
evidence_snippet: Lines 420-425: authors state the conjecture that S_{E(m)} is semistable for m≫0 and call it a 'guess'; preceding text shows partial results but not full proof.

title: There are infinitely many Hilbert cubes of dimension 3 in the set of squares
body_tex: For each $n\in\N_{+}$, there is a Hilbert cube $H(a_{0}; a_{1}, a_{2}, a_{3})\subset\cal{S}$ of dimension 3 with $a_{0}=n^2$.
plain_text: For each $n in _ + $, there is a Hilbert cube $H(a_ 0 ; a_ 1 , a_ 2 , a_ 3 ) subset S$ of dimension 3 with $a_ 0 =n^2$.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: Existence of 3‑dimensional Hilbert cubes anchored at every square is a concrete combinatorial/Diophantine existence problem, numerically supported but narrowly focused within additive number theory.
viability_rationale: Strong computational evidence and lower-bound results exist (many cubes); full uniform existence for every base square is plausible but likely requires new constructive/Diophantine techniques.
rationale: Authors formulate as a conjecture (existence for every n) based on computational data and do not prove it; paper proves lower bounds but conjecture remains open.
evidence_snippet: Lines 1202-1204: conjecture stated. Lines 1207-1210: authors proved H_3(N) ≫ N^{1/8} and give computational evidence, not a proof of the conjecture.

title: On the triviality of inhomogeneous deformations of $\mathfrak{osp}(1|2n)$
plain_text: For $B(m,n) = (2m+1|2n)$ with $m + n >= 2$, the $$-deformation is trivial if and only if $ = 0$.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: Natural extension of the proved osp(1|2n) triviality to general oscillator Lie superalgebras; meaningful in deformation and representation theory but relatively specialized.
viability_rationale: Paper proves B(0,n), small (m,n) cases checked computationally; main hurdle is algebraic coefficients (Q(√2)) and extending certificate construction. Rank-invariance and explicit methods give a concrete path, so resolution in ~5 years is plausible.
rationale: Authors conjecture extension to general B(m,n); they proved B(0,n) case and verified small m,n computationally, but did not prove the general statement.
evidence_snippet: This conjecture has been computationally verified for small m,n ... For m\ge 1, the deformation parameters involve coefficients in \mathbb{Q}(\sqrt{2}) ... generalizing the proof requires rank invariance.

title: Formal specification and behavioral simulation of the holiday gift exchange game
body_tex: Under standard gift exchange rules with correlated valuations, the coalition formation game has a non-empty core. In particular, family-sized coalitions ($|K| \in \{2, 3, 4\}$) among players with correlated preferences are stable.
plain_text: Under standard gift exchange rules with correlated valuations, the coalition formation game has a non-empty core. In particular, family-sized coalitions ($|K| in 2, 3, 4 $) among players with correlated preferences are stable.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: Model-specific coalition stability claim in a gift-exchange game; of interest to applied game theory and experimental work but narrow and dependent on model assumptions.
viability_rationale: Specific finite cooperative-game claim for small coalitions; amenable to computational enumeration or constructive proofs, so resolution within 5 years plausible. Ambiguity and general correlated-valuation quantification reduce certainty.
rationale: The non-empty core statement for the gift exchange coalition game is posed by the author as a conjecture and not proven in the paper.
evidence_snippet: \begin{conjecture}[Non-empty core] Under standard gift exchange rules with correlated valuations, the coalition formation game has a non-empty core. ... \end{conjecture}

title: On the structure of approximate rings
body_tex:
\label{conjecture: bound on N_2(K)}
$N_2(K)$ can be chosen to be polynomially bounded, i.e. $N_2(K) \leq C K^d$ for some constants $C$ and $d$ which do not depend on $K$.
plain_text: $N_2(K)$ can be chosen to be polynomially bounded, i.e. $N_2(K) <= C K^d$ for some constants $C$ and $d$ which do not depend on $K$.
label: real_open_conjecture
interestingness_rationale: A quantitative (polynomial) bound on a commensurability parameter in the finite approximate subring structure theorem; technically useful in additive combinatorics but a specialized, quantitative strengthening.
viability_rationale: Polynomial bounds mirror a major open problem for approximate groups; authors state it seems out of reach of current methods, so near-term resolution unlikely.
rationale: The authors pose the polynomial bound on N_2(K) as an explicit conjecture, remarking it is out of reach of current methods; it is not proved in the paper.
evidence_snippet: N_2(K) can be chosen to be polynomially bounded, i.e. N_2(K) ≤ C K^d for some constants C and d. (Conjecture; 'seems to be out of reach of the current methods')

Assume that, for every cycle $i_1,,i_k,i_ k+1 $, we have equation* _ l=1 ^ k f_ i_l,i_ l+1 () = _ l=1 ^ k f_ i_ l+1 ,i_l () equation* for a.e. $ in [-,]$, where $i_ k+1 =i_1$. Then, equation* A_n _n _ bmatrix f_ 1,1 & f_ 1,2 & & f_ 1,k f_ 2,1 & f_ 2,2 & & & & & f_ k-1 , k f_ k,1 & & f_ k, k-1 & f_ k, k bmatrix. equation*
